     1Ôćĺ#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2Ôćĺ"""
     3ÔćĺPurpose:   Emit rdf:type triples for every DDB object in the Goethe-Faust corpus.
     4Ôćĺ           Classification rule (OR): sector-2 (sparte002) OR htype signals
     5Ôćĺ           manifestation Ôćĺ rda:Manifestation (rdaregistry C10007); else Ôćĺ
     6Ôćĺ           mocho:Manifestation.
     7ÔćĺUsage:     python gen_manifestation_types.py [--jsonl FILE] [--out FILE]
     8ÔćĺInputs:    data/items-all-goethe-faust.json   JSONL, one record per line
     9ÔćĺOutputs:   output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt       N-Triples
    10ÔćĺDeps:      stdlib only (json, argparse, pathlib)
    11ÔćĺAssumes:   JSONL: one JSON object per line; record structure edm.RDF.ProvidedCHO
    12Ôćĺ"""
    13Ôćĺ
    14Ôćĺimport argparse
    15Ôćĺimport json
    16Ôćĺimport sys
    17Ôćĺfrom pathlib import Path
    18Ôćĺ
    19ÔćĺSCRIPT_DIR  = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
    20ÔćĺPROJECT_DIR = SCRIPT_DIR.parent
    21Ôćĺ
    22ÔćĺDEFAULT_JSONL = PROJECT_DIR / "data"   / "items-all-goethe-faust.json"
    23ÔćĺDEFAULT_OUT   = Path("/Users/mta/Documents/claude/ddbkg/goethe-faust/data") / "mocho-goethe-faust.nt"
    24Ôćĺ
    25ÔćĺRDF_TYPE       = "http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type"
    26ÔćĺRDA_MANIFEST   = "http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/C10007"
    27ÔćĺMOCHO_MANIFEST = "https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#Manifestation"
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ# htype codes that signal a manifestation-level object
    30ÔćĺHTYPE_MANIFEST = {
    31Ôćĺ    "htype_007",   # Band / Volume
    32Ôćĺ    "htype_013",   # Handschrift / Manuscript
    33Ôćĺ    "htype_014",   # Heft / Issue
    34Ôćĺ    "htype_020",   # Mehrb├Ąndiges Werk / Multivolume Work
    35Ôćĺ    "htype_021",   # Monografie / Monograph
    36Ôćĺ    "htype_025",   # Rezension / Review
    37Ôćĺ}
    38Ôćĺ
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺdef is_sector2(item: dict) -> bool:
    41Ôćĺ    """Return True if the item or any parent institution belongs to sparte002."""
    42Ôćĺ    pi = item.get("provider-info") or {}
    43Ôćĺ    domains: list = pi.get("domains") or []
    44Ôćĺ    parents: list = (pi.get("provider-parents") or {}).get("parents") or []
    45Ôćĺ    parent_domains = [d for p in parents for d in (p.get("domains") or [])]
    46Ôćĺ    all_domains = domains + parent_domains
    47Ôćĺ    return any("sparte002" in (d or "") for d in all_domains)
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺdef classify(item: dict) -> str:
    51Ôćĺ    cho   = (item.get("edm") or {}).get("RDF", {}).get("ProvidedCHO") or {}
    52Ôćĺ    htype = cho.get("hierarchyType")
    53Ôćĺ    if is_sector2(item) or htype in HTYPE_MANIFEST:
    54Ôćĺ        return RDA_MANIFEST
    55Ôćĺ    return MOCHO_MANIFEST
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺdef nt_triple(subject: str, predicate: str, obj: str) -> str:
    59Ôćĺ    return f"<{subject}> <{predicate}> <{obj}> .\n"
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺdef main() -> None:
    63Ôćĺ    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate rdf:type N-Triples for Goethe-Faust corpus")
    64Ôćĺ    parser.add_argument("--jsonl", type=Path, default=DEFAULT_JSONL, help="Input JSONL file")
    65Ôćĺ    parser.add_argument("--out",   type=Path, default=DEFAULT_OUT,   help="Output .nt file")
    66Ôćĺ    args = parser.parse_args()
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ    if not args.jsonl.exists():
    69Ôćĺ        sys.exit(f"Input file not found: {args.jsonl}")
    70Ôćĺ
    71Ôćĺ    args.out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ    total = skipped = rda_sector = rda_htype = mocho_count = 0
    74Ôćĺ
    75Ôćĺ    with args.jsonl.open(encoding="utf-8") as fin, \
    76Ôćĺ         args.out.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as fout:
    77Ôćĺ
    78Ôćĺ        for lineno, line in enumerate(fin, 1):
    79Ôćĺ            line = line.strip()
    80Ôćĺ            if not line:
    81Ôćĺ                continue
    82Ôćĺ            try:
    83Ôćĺ                item = json.loads(line)
    84Ôćĺ            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
    85Ôćĺ                print(f"  WARNING line {lineno}: JSON parse error ÔÇö {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
    86Ôćĺ                skipped += 1
    87Ôćĺ                continue
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ            try:
    90Ôćĺ                cho = (item.get("edm") or {}).get("RDF", {}).get("ProvidedCHO")
    91Ôćĺ                if not cho:
    92Ôćĺ                    raise ValueError("missing ProvidedCHO")
    93Ôćĺ                uri = cho.get("about")
    94Ôćĺ                if not uri:
    95Ôćĺ                    raise ValueError("missing ProvidedCHO.about")
    96Ôćĺ            except (KeyError, ValueError) as exc:
    97Ôćĺ                print(f"  WARNING line {lineno}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
    98Ôćĺ                skipped += 1
    99Ôćĺ                continue
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ            htype = cho.get("hierarchyType")
   102Ôćĺ            sector2 = is_sector2(item)
   103Ôćĺ            htype_m = htype in HTYPE_MANIFEST
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ            if sector2 or htype_m:
   106Ôćĺ                cls = RDA_MANIFEST
   107Ôćĺ                if sector2:
   108Ôćĺ                    rda_sector += 1
   109Ôćĺ                else:
   110Ôćĺ                    rda_htype += 1
   111Ôćĺ            else:
   112Ôćĺ                cls = MOCHO_MANIFEST
   113Ôćĺ                mocho_count += 1
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ            fout.write(nt_triple(uri, RDF_TYPE, cls))
   116Ôćĺ            total += 1
   117Ôćĺ
   118Ôćĺ    print(f"Done.")
   119Ôćĺ    print(f"  Total triples written : {total:,}")
   120Ôćĺ    print(f"  rda:Manifestation     : {rda_sector + rda_htype:,}  "
   121Ôćĺ          f"(sector2: {rda_sector:,}, htype: {rda_htype:,})")
   122Ôćĺ    print(f"  mocho:Manifestation   : {mocho_count:,}")
   123Ôćĺ    print(f"  Skipped               : {skipped:,}")
   124Ôćĺ    print(f"  Output                : {args.out}")
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ
   127Ôćĺif __name__ == "__main__":
   128Ôćĺ    main()
   129Ôćĺ



```

The four source config files are ready:\n- `scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json` ÔÇö audio dc:types Ôćĺ MO/ACO classes (positional [W,E,M,I,carrier])\n- `scripts/old-config/type2class.json` ÔÇö general text dc:types Ôćĺ FaBiO classes (positional [W,E,M,I])\n- `output/config/image_type2class.json` ÔÇö photo dc:types Ôćĺ VRA/LIO classes (keyed as `dc_type_de__sparteXXX`)\n- `output/config/video_type2class.json` ÔÇö video dc:types Ôćĺ EBUCore Plus classes (key = dc_type_de, `sectors` list)\n\nTask B1: write `scripts/gen_dctype_class_mapping.py` Ôćĺ outputs `output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv`.\n\n## Output schema\n\n`lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` columns:\n`mediatype, sector, dc_type_de, dc_type_en, rdf_type_m, rdf_type_e, rdf_type_w, rdf_type_i, source_vocab, notes`\n\nLookup priority (used later in `retype_entities()`):\n1. Exact (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de)\n2. (mediatype, `any`, dc_type_de)\n3. (`any`, `any`, dc_type_de)\n4. No match Ôćĺ no additional type\n\n## CLASS_MAP (old string Ôćĺ full IRI)\n\n```python\nCLASS_MAP = {\n    \"mo:MusicalWork\":           \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalWork\",\n    \"mo:MusicalManifestation\":  \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalManifestation\",\n    \"mo:MusicalExpression\":     \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalExpression\",\n    \"mo:MuscialExpression\":     \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalExpression\",  # typo fix\n    \"mo:Record\":                \"http://purl.org/ontology/mo/Record\",\n    \"aco:AudioManifestation\":   \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioManifestation\",\n    \"aco:AudioClip\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioClip\",\n    \"aco:AudioExpression\":      \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioExpression\",\n    \"aco:AudioItem\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioItem\",\n    \"aco:AudioFile\":            \"https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioFile\",\n    \"vra:Image\":                \"http://purl.org/vra/Image\",\n    \"vra:Work\":                 \"http://purl.org/vra/Work\",\n    \"lio:Image\":                \"https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image\",\n    \"ebucoreplus:EditorialWork\":   \"http://www.ebu.ch/metadata/ontologies/ebucoreplus#EditorialWork\",\n    \"ebucoreplus:MediaResource\":   \"http://www.ebu.ch/metadata/ontologies/ebucoreplus#MediaResource\",\n    \"rico:Record\":              \"http://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#Record\",\n    \"doco:Section\":             \"http://purl.org/spar/doco/Section\",\n    \"fabio:AudioDocument\":      \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/AudioDocument\",\n    # Generic FaBiO/FRBR carriers Ôćĺ drop (emit empty string)\n    \"fabio:PrintObject\":        \"\",  \"fabio:AnalogItem\":     \"\",\n    \"fabio:DigitalItem\":        \"\",  \"fabio:Work\":           \"\",\n    \"fabio:WorkCollection\":     \"\",  \"fabio:Textbook\":       \"\",\n    \"fabio:MastersThesis\":      \"\",  \"fabio:ReportDocument\": \"\",\n    \"fabio:NewspaperArticle\":   \"\",  \"fabio:AudioDocument\":  \"http://purl.org/spar/fabio/AudioDocument\",\n    \"frbr:Work\":                \"\",  \"frbr:Manifestation\":   \"\",\n    \"frbr:Expression\":          \"\",  \"frbr:Item\":            \"\",\n}\n```\n\n## Implementation steps\n\n### 1. Audio (`audio_type2class.json`)\n\n- Skip key `\"Muster\"` (template).\n- `sector` field in old-config is an integer (4, 6) or absent Ôćĺ convert:\n  - `4 Ôćĺ http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte004`\n  - `6 Ôćĺ http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/sparte006`\n  - absent Ôćĺ `any`\n- `class` array is positional: index 0=W, 1=E, 2=M, 3=I (index 4=carrier, ignored).\n- Index 2 (M slot) may be a list Ôćĺ take first non-empty after CLASS_MAP; if `mo:Record` or `aco:AudioClip` appear together, use `aco:AudioManifestation` (per ┬ž3.1 rule).\n- Apply CLASS_MAP to all slots; skip empty strings and unmapped deprecated classes.\n- `mediatype = http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt001`\n- `source_vocab`: derive from the resolved IRI prefix (`mo`, `aco`, `fabio`).\n- `dc_type_en` from `\"en\"` field; `notes` from `\"remarks\"` field.\n\n**Special M-slot rule for audio**: if M-slot list contains `mo:Record` + `aco:AudioClip` (non-musical default), map to `aco:AudioManifestation` instead (per plan ┬ž3.1). Only emit `aco:AudioClip` if dc:type denotes a clip/excerpt.\n\n### 2. Text (`type2class.json`)\n\n- Keys may have prefixes: strip leading `:`, `!gnd_id!`, `[`, `(` patterns to get clean `dc_type_de`.\n- class array: index 0=W, 1=E, 2=M, 3=I.\n- All FaBiO/FRBR classes map to `\"\"` in CLASS_MAP Ôćĺ these rows get empty `rdf_type_m`.\n- `mediatype = any`, `sector = any`.\n- `source_vocab = rda` (or `rico` if applicable; empty otherwise since most text entries will have no mapped class).\n- Still emit the row (dc_type_de + dc_type_en captured for reference, even if rdf_type_m is empty).\n\n### 3. Photo (`image_type2class.json`)\n\n- Key format: `dc_type_de + \"__\" + sector_short` (e.g. `Zeichnung__sparte006`).\n- Split on `__` to recover dc_type_de and sector_short Ôćĺ expand sector_short to full IRI.\n- class array index 2 (M slot) may be a list Ôćĺ apply CLASS_MAP to each, keep non-empty.\n- If list has both `vra:Image` and `lio:Image` Ôćĺ emit both as comma-separated in `rdf_type_m`?\n  - **Decision**: emit primary as `rdf_type_m`; secondary in `notes`. Keep one IRI per cell for simplicity in the transform lookup.\n  - Primary: `lio:Image` for sparte005 (museum), `vra:Image` otherwise.\n- `mediatype = http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt002`.\n- `source_vocab = vra` or `lio` or `rico`.\n\n### 4. Video (`video_type2class.json`)\n\n- Key = dc_type_de; `sectors` field is a list of short codes (e.g. `[\"sparte005\"]`).\n- Expand each sector short code to full IRI.\n- Emit one row per sector.\n- class array index 2 (M slot) Ôćĺ apply CLASS_MAP.\n- `mediatype = http://ddb.vocnet.org/medientyp/mt005`.\n- `source_vocab = ebucoreplus`.\n\n### 5. Write CSV\n\n- Collect all rows in a list; sort by (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de).\n- Write with `csv.DictWriter`, fieldnames per ┬ž2 schema.\n- Print summary: total rows, rows with non-empty `rdf_type_m`, rows where `rdf_type_m` is empty.\n\n## Critical files\n\n| File | Role |\n|---|---|\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/gen_dctype_class_mapping.py` | **New script** ÔÇö write this |\n| `goethe-faust/output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` | **New output** |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/audio_type2class.json` | Source |\n| `goethe-faust/scripts/old-config/type2class.json` | Source |\n| `goethe-faust/output/config/image_type2class.json` | Source |\n| `goethe-faust/output/config/video_type2class.json` | Source |\n\n## Open question before writing\n\nOne ambiguity in the photo sector handling: `image_type2class.json` has entries with `[\"vra:Image\", \"lio:Image\"]` in the M-slot list. The CSV schema has one `rdf_type_m` column. Plan is: emit primary IRI in `rdf_type_m`, secondary in `notes`. Primary selection: `lio:Image` for museum (sparte005), `vra:Image` otherwise. **Is this correct, or should both IRIs be emitted (comma-separated in rdf_type_m)?**\n\n## Verification (B2ÔÇôB3)\n\n1. Run `python scripts/gen_dctype_class_mapping.py`\n2. Check summary output: row count, empty-rdf_type_m count\n3. `grep TBD output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv` Ôćĺ must return nothing\n4. Spot-check: `Schallplatte` Ôćĺ `rdf_type_m = http://purl.org/ontology/mo/MusicalManifestation`\n5. Spot-check: `Audiofile` Ôćĺ `rdf_type_m = https://w3id.org/ac-ontology/aco#AudioManifestation`\n"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-04-17T08:41:55.876Z)*

Now I have the full picture. Let me write the plan.

**Key finding from the corpus + PDFs:**

- `sparte005` top mt002 types: `Fotografie` (1898), `Druck` (834), `Zeichnung` (160) ÔÇö mix of photographic artifacts and artworks
- `sparte006` top mt002 types: `Zeichnung` (3167), `Druckgraphik` (2576), `Gem├Ąlde` (549) ÔÇö almost all artworks
- VRA makes a hard distinction: `vra:Work` (the physical artwork) vs `vra:Image` (documentation of the work)
- Current `image_type2class.json` maps Zeichnung, Druckgraphik, Gem├Ąlde Ôćĺ `vra:Image` ÔÇö **this is wrong**: these are artworks (`vra:Work`), not documentation images

The plan needs to correct this and resolve the two-IRI (`vra:Image` + `lio:Image`) schema question.

### Assistant *(2026-04-17T08:42:39.616Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/.claude/plans/wobbly-swimming-moon.md",
  "content": "# Plan: Map DDB Museum Objects with `edm:type = \"IMAGE\"` to mocho (mt002)\n\n## Context\n\nDDB records with `edm:type = \"IMAGE\"` and `mediatype = mt002` span two ontologically\ndistinct categories in VRA Core / LIO:\n\n1. **Photographic artifacts** ÔÇö the CHO IS a photograph, negative, or slide. The record\n   describes the photo as a cultural object. Ôćĺ `vra:Image`\n2. **Visual artworks** ÔÇö the CHO is a drawing, print, or painting. The record describes\n   the physical artwork. The digital scan attached via `edm:isShownBy` is the image of\n   it, not the CHO itself. Ôćĺ `vra:Work`\n\nThe current `image_type2class.json` maps both categories to `vra:Image`. This is\nincorrect for artwork dc:types (Zeichnung, Druckgraphik, Gem├Ąlde, Malerei, etc.)\nwhich should be `vra:Work`.\n\n**Corpus distribution (top dc:types by count)**:\n\n| sector | dc:type | count | correct class |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| sparte005 | Fotografie | 1898 | `vra:Image` + `lio:Image` |\n| sparte005 | Druck | 834 | `vra:Image` (photographic print in museum) |\n| sparte005 | Zeichnung | 160 | `vra:Work` |\n| sparte005 | Standfoto | 103 | `vra:Image` + `lio:Image` |\n| sparte006 | Zeichnung | 3167 | `vra:Work` |\n| sparte006 | Druckgraphik | 2576 | `vra:Work` |\n| sparte006 | Gem├Ąlde | 549 | `vra:Work` |\n| sparte006 | Fotografie | 150 | `vra:Image` |\n\n---\n\n## 1. VRA Core + LIO conceptual mapping\n\n**VRA Work vs Image** (rm023-vra.pdf):\n- `vra:Work` ÔÇö the physical/conceptual artwork (drawing, painting, print). Central class.\n  Properties: creator, material, technique, stylePeriod, culturalContext, dateCreated.\n- `vra:Image` ÔÇö the image documentation of a work. Connected to `vra:Work` via `vra:imageOf`.\n- These are separate classes; a DDB CHO is one or the other, not both.\n\n**LIO three-layer model** (rm024-lio.pdf, Hayes & Warren 2010):\n- Layer 1: **object** (physical artifact ÔÇö furniture, person, artwork)\n- Layer 2: **photo of the object** (the CHO for photographic museum records)\n- Layer 3: **scan of the photo** (the `edm:WebResource` / `edm:isShownBy` target)\n- `foaf:Image` = the subject image (the digital scan, layer 3) ÔÇö this is NOT the ProvidedCHO\n- `lio:fascimile` = \"sameImageAs\", relates layer 3 to layer 2\n- `lio:depicts` = what the image is about (links image to subject terms, people, events)\n\n**Critical insight**: `lio:Image` (= `foaf:Image` in mocho-edit.owl) applies to the\n*digital representation*, not necessarily to the ProvidedCHO. However, mocho uses\n`lio:Image` alongside `vra:Image` for museum photographic records because the CHO\nis itself the digitised photo (layers 2 and 3 collapse in the DDB metadata model).\n\n---\n\n## 2. dc:type taxonomy for mt002\n\n### 2.1 Photographic types Ôćĺ `vra:Image` (+ `lio:Image` for sparte005)\n\nThe DDB record describes a photographic artifact. The CHO is the photograph, negative,\nslide, or scan.\n\n```\nFotografie, Foto, Standfoto, Kontaktbogen, Kleinbilddia, Bilddokument,\nFotomechanische Reproduktion, Fotoalbum (as a collection of photos),\nPostkarte (photographic postcard)\n```\n\nType assignment:\n- `rdf_type_m = vra:Image` (IRI: `http://purl.org/vra/Image`)\n- For sparte005 (Museum): also emit `lio:Image` (IRI: `https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image`)\n- For sparte001 (Archive): also emit `rico:Record` (archival photograph)\n\n### 2.2 Artwork types Ôćĺ `vra:Work`\n\nThe DDB record describes a physical visual artwork. The digital image is documentation\nof the work, not the work itself.\n\n```\nZeichnung, Druckgraphik, Druckgrafik, Druckschrift, Gem├Ąlde, Malerei,\nGrafik, Graphik, Kunst, Wandbild, Druck (in sparte006 context),\nMappenwerk, Plakat (as designed artwork), Geldschein, Medaille\n```\n\nType assignment:\n- `rdf_type_m = vra:Work` (IRI: `http://purl.org/vra/Work`)\n- No `lio:Image` ÔÇö the CHO is not an image, it is an object\n\n### 2.3 Ambiguous types ÔÇö sector-dependent\n\n| dc:type | sparte005 | sparte006 |\n|---|---|---|\n| Druck | `vra:Image` (photographic print) | `vra:Work` (printed artwork) |\n| Album Zeichnung | `vra:Work` (collection of drawings) | `vra:Work` |\n| Plakat | `vra:Work` (designed poster) | `vra:Work` |\n\n---\n\n## 3. Two-IRI problem: `vra:Image` + `lio:Image`\n\n**Problem**: CSV schema has one `rdf_type_m` column but some entries need two IRIs.\n\n**Decision**: Allow multiple rows per (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de) triple.\n- Relax ┬ž2 uniqueness constraint: lookup is a list of matches, not a single match\n- `retype_entities()` iterates ALL matching rows and accumulates rdf:type IRIs\n- Dedup at emit time (already in the dispatch model, ┬ž6 accumulate+dedup)\n\nThis is consistent with the existing accumulate model and avoids schema changes.\n\nExample rows for `Fotografie` + sparte005:\n```\nmt002, sparte005, Fotografie, Photograph, http://purl.org/vra/Image, ...\nmt002, sparte005, Fotografie, Photograph, https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image, ...\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. Required corrections to `image_type2class.json`\n\nThe following entries are currently mapped to `vra:Image` but should be `vra:Work`.\n**These must be corrected before running `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py`**:\n\n| Key | Current | Correct |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Zeichnung__sparte005` | `vra:Image` | `vra:Work` |\n| `Zeichnung__sparte006` | `vra:Image` | `vra:Work` |\n| `Druckgraphik__sparte006` | `vra:Image` | `vra:Work` |\n| Any key with `Gem├Ąlde`, `Malerei`, `Grafik`, `Graphik` | `vra:Image` | `vra:Work` |\n\nAlso: add missing rows for photographic types that need `lio:Image` (second row per key\nfor sparte005 photographic types).\n\n**Approach**: Do NOT hand-edit `image_type2class.json`. Instead, update\n`gen_image_type2class.py` to:\n- Classify dc:types as `ARTWORK_TYPES` (Ôćĺ `vra:Work`) vs `PHOTO_TYPES` (Ôćĺ `vra:Image`)\n- For `PHOTO_TYPES` in sparte005: emit a second row with `lio:Image`\n- Regenerate the file\n\n---\n\n## 5. `vra:imageOf` property (out of scope for this plan)\n\nVRA links `vra:Image` to `vra:Work` via `vra:imageOf`. In the DDB model:\n- A photographic CHO (layer 2) depicts the physical object (layer 1)\n- The depiction link could be expressed as `vra:imageOf` pointing to the depicted\n  entity (from dc:subject, dc:relation, or a separate CHO for the depicted object)\n\nThis is a property alignment question, not a rdf:type dispatch question. **Not in scope\nhere**; handled by a future property alignment pass or GND enrichment phase.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Files to modify\n\n| File | Action |\n|---|---|\n| `scripts/gen_image_type2class.py` | Add ARTWORK_TYPES / PHOTO_TYPES dicts; fix vra:Work vs vra:Image; add lio:Image second rows |\n| `output/config/image_type2class.json` | Regenerated output |\n| `notes/transform-script-plan.md` ┬ž3.3 | Update with corrected taxonomy |\n| `notes/alignment-adr.md` | Add ADR entry for vra:Work vs vra:Image dispatch decision |\n\n`gen_dctype_class_mapping.py` is NOT changed ÔÇö it reads from the corrected\n`image_type2class.json` and emits multiple rows per key wherever needed.\nThe ┬ž2 uniqueness note in the plan is updated to allow multiple rows per key.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Verification\n\n1. Run corrected `gen_image_type2class.py` Ôćĺ inspect `output/config/image_type2class.json`\n2. Spot-check: `Zeichnung__sparte006` Ôćĺ `vra:Work`; `Fotografie__sparte005` Ôćĺ two entries (`vra:Image`, `lio:Image`)\n3. Run `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py` Ôćĺ inspect `output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv`\n4. Check: no Zeichnung/Druckgraphik/Gem├Ąlde row has `rdf_type_m = vra:Image`\n5. Check: `Fotografie` + sparte005 produces two rdf:type rows in the transform output\n6. Run `sample_type_dispatch.py` (Phase C); spot-check museum photo records\n\n---\n\n## Open question before implementing ┬ž4\n\n`gen_image_type2class.py` needs a `ARTWORK_TYPES` set. The full list of mt002 dc:types\nin sparte005/006 is large (695 unique values). The cleanest approach:\n- Maintain an explicit `ARTWORK_TYPES` set for known artwork dc:types\n- Default rule: if dc:type not in `ARTWORK_TYPES` Ôćĺ `vra:Image`\n- OR: maintain explicit `PHOTO_TYPES` set; default Ôćĺ `vra:Work` (since most mt002\n  records are artwork in sparte006)\n\n**Which default makes more sense given the corpus?** sparte006 is dominated by\nartworks (Zeichnung 3167, Druckgraphik 2576); sparte005 is mixed. Recommend:\n- sparte006 default Ôćĺ `vra:Work`\n- sparte005 default Ôćĺ `vra:Image`\n"
}

The record\n   describes the photo as a cultural object. Ôćĺ `vra:Image`\n2. **Visual artworks** ÔÇö the CHO is a drawing, print, or painting. The record describes\n   the physical artwork. The digital scan attached via `edm:isShownBy` is the image of\n   it, not the CHO itself. Ôćĺ `vra:Work`\n\nThe current `image_type2class.json` maps both categories to `vra:Image`. This is\nincorrect for artwork dc:types (Zeichnung, Druckgraphik, Gem├Ąlde, Malerei, etc.)\nwhich should be `vra:Work`.\n\n**Corpus distribution (top dc:types by count)**:\n\n| sector | dc:type | count | correct class |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| sparte005 | Fotografie | 1898 | `vra:Image` + `lio:Image` |\n| sparte005 | Druck | 834 | `vra:Image` (photographic print in museum) |\n| sparte005 | Zeichnung | 160 | `vra:Work` |\n| sparte005 | Standfoto | 103 | `vra:Image` + `lio:Image` |\n| sparte006 | Zeichnung | 3167 | `vra:Work` |\n| sparte006 | Druckgraphik | 2576 | `vra:Work` |\n| sparte006 | Gem├Ąlde | 549 | `vra:Work` |\n| sparte006 | Fotografie | 150 | `vra:Image` |\n\n---\n\n## 1. VRA Core + LIO conceptual mapping\n\n**VRA Work vs Image** (rm023-vra.pdf):\n- `vra:Work` ÔÇö the physical/conceptual artwork (drawing, painting, print). Central class.\n  Properties: creator, material, technique, stylePeriod, culturalContext, dateCreated.\n- `vra:Image` ÔÇö the image documentation of a work. Connected to `vra:Work` via `vra:imageOf`.\n- These are separate classes; a DDB CHO is one or the other, not both.\n\n**LIO three-layer model** (rm024-lio.pdf, Hayes & Warren 2010):\n- Layer 1: **object** (physical artifact ÔÇö furniture, person, artwork)\n- Layer 2: **photo of the object** (the CHO for photographic museum records)\n- Layer 3: **scan of the photo** (the `edm:WebResource` / `edm:isShownBy` target)\n- `foaf:Image` = the subject image (the digital scan, layer 3) ÔÇö this is NOT the ProvidedCHO\n- `lio:fascimile` = \"sameImageAs\", relates layer 3 to layer 2\n- `lio:depicts` = what the image is about (links image to subject terms, people, events)\n\n**Critical insight**: `lio:Image` (= `foaf:Image` in mocho-edit.owl) applies to the\n*digital representation*, not necessarily to the ProvidedCHO. However, mocho uses\n`lio:Image` alongside `vra:Image` for museum photographic records because the CHO\nis itself the digitised photo (layers 2 and 3 collapse in the DDB metadata model).\n\n---\n\n## 2. dc:type taxonomy for mt002\n\n### 2.1 Photographic types Ôćĺ `vra:Image` (+ `lio:Image` for sparte005)\n\nThe DDB record describes a photographic artifact. The CHO is the photograph, negative,\nslide, or scan.\n\n```\nFotografie, Foto, Standfoto, Kontaktbogen, Kleinbilddia, Bilddokument,\nFotomechanische Reproduktion, Fotoalbum (as a collection of photos),\nPostkarte (photographic postcard)\n```\n\nType assignment:\n- `rdf_type_m = vra:Image` (IRI: `http://purl.org/vra/Image`)\n- For sparte005 (Museum): also emit `lio:Image` (IRI: `https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image`)\n- For sparte001 (Archive): also emit `rico:Record` (archival photograph)\n\n### 2.2 Artwork types Ôćĺ `vra:Work`\n\nThe DDB record describes a physical visual artwork. The digital image is documentation\nof the work, not the work itself.\n\n```\nZeichnung, Druckgraphik, Druckgrafik, Druckschrift, Gem├Ąlde, Malerei,\nGrafik, Graphik, Kunst, Wandbild, Druck (in sparte006 context),\nMappenwerk, Plakat (as designed artwork), Geldschein, Medaille\n```\n\nType assignment:\n- `rdf_type_m = vra:Work` (IRI: `http://purl.org/vra/Work`)\n- No `lio:Image` ÔÇö the CHO is not an image, it is an object\n\n### 2.3 Ambiguous types ÔÇö sector-dependent\n\n| dc:type | sparte005 | sparte006 |\n|---|---|---|\n| Druck | `vra:Image` (photographic print) | `vra:Work` (printed artwork) |\n| Album Zeichnung | `vra:Work` (collection of drawings) | `vra:Work` |\n| Plakat | `vra:Work` (designed poster) | `vra:Work` |\n\n---\n\n## 3. Two-IRI problem: `vra:Image` + `lio:Image`\n\n**Problem**: CSV schema has one `rdf_type_m` column but some entries need two IRIs.\n\n**Decision**: Allow multiple rows per (mediatype, sector, dc_type_de) triple.\n- Relax ┬ž2 uniqueness constraint: lookup is a list of matches, not a single match\n- `retype_entities()` iterates ALL matching rows and accumulates rdf:type IRIs\n- Dedup at emit time (already in the dispatch model, ┬ž6 accumulate+dedup)\n\nThis is consistent with the existing accumulate model and avoids schema changes.\n\nExample rows for `Fotografie` + sparte005:\n```\nmt002, sparte005, Fotografie, Photograph, http://purl.org/vra/Image, ...\nmt002, sparte005, Fotografie, Photograph, https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image, ...\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. Required corrections to `image_type2class.json`\n\nThe following entries are currently mapped to `vra:Image` but should be `vra:Work`.\n**These must be corrected before running `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py`**:\n\n| Key | Current | Correct |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Zeichnung__sparte005` | `vra:Image` | `vra:Work` |\n| `Zeichnung__sparte006` | `vra:Image` | `vra:Work` |\n| `Druckgraphik__sparte006` | `vra:Image` | `vra:Work` |\n| Any key with `Gem├Ąlde`, `Malerei`, `Grafik`, `Graphik` | `vra:Image` | `vra:Work` |\n\nAlso: add missing rows for photographic types that need `lio:Image` (second row per key\nfor sparte005 photographic types).\n\n**Approach**: Do NOT hand-edit `image_type2class.json`. Instead, update\n`gen_image_type2class.py` to:\n- Classify dc:types as `ARTWORK_TYPES` (Ôćĺ `vra:Work`) vs `PHOTO_TYPES` (Ôćĺ `vra:Image`)\n- For `PHOTO_TYPES` in sparte005: emit a second row with `lio:Image`\n- Regenerate the file\n\n---\n\n## 5. `vra:imageOf` property (out of scope for this plan)\n\nVRA links `vra:Image` to `vra:Work` via `vra:imageOf`. In the DDB model:\n- A photographic CHO (layer 2) depicts the physical object (layer 1)\n- The depiction link could be expressed as `vra:imageOf` pointing to the depicted\n  entity (from dc:subject, dc:relation, or a separate CHO for the depicted object)\n\nThis is a property alignment question, not a rdf:type dispatch question. **Not in scope\nhere**; handled by a future property alignment pass or GND enrichment phase.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Files to modify\n\n| File | Action |\n|---|---|\n| `scripts/gen_image_type2class.py` | Add ARTWORK_TYPES / PHOTO_TYPES dicts; fix vra:Work vs vra:Image; add lio:Image second rows |\n| `output/config/image_type2class.json` | Regenerated output |\n| `notes/transform-script-plan.md` ┬ž3.3 | Update with corrected taxonomy |\n| `notes/alignment-adr.md` | Add ADR entry for vra:Work vs vra:Image dispatch decision |\n\n`gen_dctype_class_mapping.py` is NOT changed ÔÇö it reads from the corrected\n`image_type2class.json` and emits multiple rows per key wherever needed.\nThe ┬ž2 uniqueness note in the plan is updated to allow multiple rows per key.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Verification\n\n1. Run corrected `gen_image_type2class.py` Ôćĺ inspect `output/config/image_type2class.json`\n2. Spot-check: `Zeichnung__sparte006` Ôćĺ `vra:Work`; `Fotografie__sparte005` Ôćĺ two entries (`vra:Image`, `lio:Image`)\n3. Run `gen_dctype_class_mapping.py` Ôćĺ inspect `output/lookup_dctype_to_class.csv`\n4. Check: no Zeichnung/Druckgraphik/Gem├Ąlde row has `rdf_type_m = vra:Image`\n5. Check: `Fotografie` + sparte005 produces two rdf:type rows in the transform output\n6. Run `sample_type_dispatch.py` (Phase C); spot-check museum photo records\n\n---\n\n## 8. Default class rule (resolved)\n\nFor dc:types not in an explicit `ARTWORK_TYPES` or `PHOTO_TYPES` set:\n\n- `sparte006` Ôćĺ `vra:Work` (corpus is dominated by artworks: Zeichnung 3167, Druckgraphik 2576)\n- `sparte005` Ôćĺ `vra:Image` (corpus is dominated by photos: Fotografie 1898)\n- All other sectors Ôćĺ `vra:Image`\n\nArtwork types in sparte005 (Museum) Ôćĺ `vra:Work` only. **No `lio:Image`** for artwork\ntypes in any sector ÔÇö the CHO is a physical object, not a digital image.\n\n`lio:Image` is only emitted alongside `vra:Image` for **photographic types in sparte005**.\n\n### ARTWORK_TYPES set (minimum, extend as needed)\n\n```python\nARTWORK_TYPES = {\n    \"Zeichnung\", \"Album Zeichnung\",\n    \"Druckgraphik\", \"Druckgrafik\", \"Druckschrift\",\n    \"Grafik\", \"Graphik\",\n    \"Gem├Ąlde\", \"Malerei\",\n    \"Wandbild\",\n    \"Mappenwerk\",\n    \"Plakat\",       # designed poster (both sectors)\n    \"Geldschein\", \"Medaille\",  # sparte006 artifacts\n    \"Kunst\",        # generic \"artwork\"\n}\n```\n\n### PHOTO_TYPES set (always Ôćĺ `vra:Image`)\n\n```python\nPHOTO_TYPES = {\n    \"Fotografie\", \"Foto\",\n    \"Standfoto\", \"Kontaktbogen\",\n    \"Kleinbilddia\",\n    \"Bilddokument\",\n    \"Fotomechanische Reproduktion\",\n    \"Druck\",        # photographic print (sparte005 context)\n}\n```\n\n`Druck` in sparte006 falls under sector-based default Ôćĺ `vra:Work`.\n",
  "planFilePath": "/Users/mta/.claude/plans/wobbly-swimming-moon.md"
}

     1Ôćĺ# ADR: EDM JSONL Ôćĺ mocho RDF Transform
     2Ôćĺ
     3Ôćĺ**Date**: 2026-04-14
     4Ôćĺ**Status**: Accepted
     5Ôćĺ**Related**: `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md`, `goethe-faust/notes/alignment-plan.md`
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ---
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ## Context
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ`transform_edm_to_mocho.py` is the reference implementation of the mocho
    12Ôćĺingest transformation. It converts DDB-EDM JSONL corpora to mocho-aligned
    13ÔćĺRDF triples, validated against the goethe-faust corpus (115,432 records).
    14ÔćĺThe decisions here are not corpus-specific: they define the canonical approach
    15Ôćĺfor the mocho ingest pipeline and will be inherited by any subsequent
    16Ôćĺcorpus transformation built on this foundation.
    17Ôćĺ
    18ÔćĺThe alignment table `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` is produced by the mocho
    19Ôćĺpipeline (`align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`); this document records decisions that
    20Ôćĺgovern how the transform script consumes ÔÇö and in some cases corrects ÔÇö that
    21Ôćĺtable.
    22Ôćĺ
    23ÔćĺDecisions D1ÔÇôD5 in `mocho/notes/alignment-ddbedm-mocho-adr.md` cover the
    24Ôćĺupstream alignment pipeline (field profiling, DCÔćĺRDA routing, IRI resolution).
    25ÔćĺDecisions D1ÔÇôD10 here cover the downstream transform (JSONL Ôćĺ RDF). The two
    26ÔćĺADRs are complementary: neither subsumes the other. This ADR takes precedence
    27Ôćĺwhere the two overlap on transform behaviour.
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺ---
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ## Decision 1: Use JSONL as input, not the NT file
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ**Decision**: Stream `items-all-goethe-faust.json` (JSONL, one JSON object per
    34Ôćĺline). The NT file `ddbedm-goethe-faust.nt` is not used.
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺ**Alternatives considered**:
    37Ôćĺ- *NT file*: Load triples from the pre-generated NT and align by predicate IRI.
    38Ôćĺ  Rejected because: (a) the NT was generated from the same JSONL and carries no
    39Ôćĺ  additional information; (b) the alignment table is keyed by `(entity_type,
    40Ôćĺ  json_key)` matching the JSON structure, not by IRI, so NT input would require
    41Ôćĺ  a reverse IRIÔćĺjson_key lookup that is neither defined nor stable.
    42Ôćĺ
    43Ôćĺ**Rationale**: JSONL streams with constant memory, preserves the `(entity_type,
    44Ôćĺjson_key)` lookup key structure, and requires only stdlib `json`. No NT indexing
    45Ôćĺphase needed.
    46Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ---
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ## Decision 2: stdlib only ÔÇö no rdflib in the transform script
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ**Decision**: The transform script uses only Python stdlib (`json`, `csv`, `re`,
    52Ôćĺ`collections`, `argparse`, `pathlib`). rdflib is not imported.
    53Ôćĺ
    54Ôćĺ**Rationale**: The transform is a data pipeline step, not an ontology processing
    55Ôćĺstep. N-Triples output can be constructed by string formatting. rdflib adds a
    56Ôćĺnon-stdlib dependency and significant startup overhead for a script that runs
    57Ôćĺover 115k records.
    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ**Scope**: This applies only to `transform_edm_to_mocho.py`. Upstream scripts
    60Ôćĺthat parse OWL or Turtle (`align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`, `gen_htype_doco_mapping.py`)
    61Ôćĺcontinue to use rdflib.
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺ---
    64Ôćĺ
    65Ôćĺ## Decision 3: htype lookup keyed by `htype_code`, pending rows skipped
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ**Decision**: `load_htype_map()` uses the `htype_code` column (e.g. `"htype_030"`)
    68Ôćĺas the lookup key. Rows where `rdf_type == "pending"` are excluded.
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ**Rationale**: Corpus inspection confirmed that `ddb:hierarchyType` is an
    71Ôćĺ`owl:DatatypeProperty` whose literal values are `htype_code` strings. Grepping
    72Ôćĺ`items-all-goethe-faust.json` for `"htype_0"` yields exactly 92,957 matches ÔÇö
    73Ôćĺequal to the `record_count` for `ProvidedCHO,hierarchyType` in the field
    74Ôćĺprofile.
    75Ôćĺ
    76ÔćĺThe original draft keyed by `label_en (lowercased)`; this was corrected when
    77Ôćĺthe literal format was confirmed.
    78Ôćĺ
    79Ôćĺ**Pending rows** (16 codes ÔÇö Monograph, Serial, Article, Annotation, Charter,
    80ÔćĺDedication, etc.) have no DoCO or RiC-O class assigned. They fall through the
    81Ôćĺhtype lookup without emitting an additional rdf:type. All ProvidedCHOs still
    82Ôćĺreceive `mocho:Manifestation` as their base type regardless (see D9); pending
    83Ôćĺhtype codes are counted in `objects_missing_specific_type` in stats output.
    84Ôćĺ
    85Ôćĺ---
    86Ôćĺ
    87Ôćĺ## Decision 4: PhysicalThing.hierarchyType skipped this pass
    88Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ**Decision**: `retype_cho()` handles `ProvidedCHO` only. `PhysicalThing`
    90Ôćĺentities (55,771 records, 48.3% coverage) also carry `hierarchyType` but are
    91Ôćĺnot retyped in this pass.
    92Ôćĺ
    93Ôćĺ**Rationale**: The scope of this POC is the CHO graph. PhysicalThing is an
    94ÔćĺEDM modelling artefact for physical carrier description; its rdf:type mapping
    95Ôćĺrequires a separate alignment decision (likely CIDOC-CRM or LRMoo). Skipping
    96Ôćĺit does not affect the correctness of the ProvidedCHO output.
    97Ôćĺ
    98Ôćĺ**Consequence**: `PhysicalThing.hierarchyType` will appear in the unmatched
    99Ôćĺkeys stats. This is expected and documented.
   100Ôćĺ
   101Ôćĺ---
   102Ôćĺ
   103Ôćĺ## Decision 5: RDF output format ÔÇö N-Triples for pipeline, Turtle for hand-authored
   104Ôćĺ
   105Ôćĺ**Decision**: Transform output (`mocho-goethe-faust.nt`) and all pipeline
   106Ôćĺintermediate files use N-Triples (`.nt`). Hand-authored files (ontology,
   107ÔćĺSPARQL update templates) use Turtle (`.ttl`). N-Quads (`.nq`) if named graph
   108Ôćĺidentity is required.
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺ**Rationale**: N-Triples are one triple per line ÔÇö grep/awk/stream-friendly,
   111Ôćĺno parser context, trivial to sort and dedup with Unix tools. The verbosity
   112Ôćĺ(full URIs repeated) is acceptable for machine-processed intermediates.
   113ÔćĺTurtle prefix compression is valuable for human-readable hand-authored files.
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺ---
   116Ôćĺ
   117Ôćĺ## Decision 6: Triple subject keys ÔÇö IRI correction + value-type dispatch
   118Ôćĺ
   119Ôćĺ**Decision**: Three JSON keys carry subject data: `dcSubject`, `dcTermsSubject`,
   120Ôćĺ`dcTermSubject`. These are handled by a dedicated `emit_subject_triples()`
   121Ôćĺfunction, not the generic alignment loop.
   122Ôćĺ
   123Ôćĺ**Background**: Corpus inspection revealed that `dcTermsSubject` was
   124Ôćĺincorrectly mapped to `dc:subject` (`http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/subject`)
   125Ôćĺin `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`. The correct IRI is `dcterms:subject`
   126Ôćĺ(`http://purl.org/dc/terms/subject`). This was a derivation error in the
   127Ôćĺalignment script's IRI resolution step; `dcTermSubject` (note: missing `s`) was
   128Ôćĺthe one correctly resolved to `dcterms:subject` via an explicit `OVERRIDES` entry
   129Ôćĺin `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`. The fix was applied directly to the CSV (42 rows:
   130Ôćĺ`edm_prefix` `dcÔćĺdcterms`, `edm_iri` corrected).
   131Ôćĺ
   132Ôćĺ**Dispatch logic**:
   133Ôćĺ- Literal value (string or lang-tagged text) Ôćĺ RDA candidates for
   134Ôćĺ  `("ProvidedCHO", "dcSubject")` ÔÇö i.e. the dc:subject path.
   135Ôćĺ- IRI value (`{"resource": ...}`) Ôćĺ RDA candidates for
   136Ôćĺ  `("ProvidedCHO", "dcTermSubject")` ÔÇö i.e. the dcterms:subject path.
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ**Deduplication**: `emit_subject_triples()` collects values from all three keys
   139Ôćĺand deduplicates `(pred_nt, obj_nt)` pairs in a per-record set before writing.
   140ÔćĺThis prevents duplicate triples when the same value appears under multiple keys
   141Ôćĺ(occurs in ~60% of records).
   142Ôćĺ
   143Ôćĺ**Rationale**: `dc:subject` is conventionally used for uncontrolled literals;
   144Ôćĺ`dcterms:subject` for IRI references to controlled vocabulary terms (GND, LCSH,
   145Ôćĺetc.). Value-type dispatch applies this convention without requiring agent-type
   146Ôćĺknowledge. The per-record set dedup is cheap (reset each record) and removes
   147Ôćĺthe problem at the source.
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ---
   150Ôćĺ
   151Ôćĺ## Decision 7: Creator fan-out ÔÇö whitelist to Manifestation-level creator property
   152Ôćĺ
   153Ôćĺ**Decision**: `dc:creator` (json_key: `creator`) is mapped to a single RDA
   154Ôćĺproperty: `rdam:P30263 has creator agent of manifestation`
   155Ôćĺ(`http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30263`). The alignment table's 464
   156ÔćĺWork-level candidates are bypassed.
   157Ôćĺ
   158Ôćĺ**Background**: The alignment table produces 464 candidates for `creator`,
   159Ôćĺall at the Work WEMI level ÔÇö including highly specific properties such as
   160Ôćĺ"has production company", "has plaintiff corporate body", "has appellee
   161Ôćĺcorporate body". These are correct sub-properties of Work-level creator
   162Ôćĺproperties in the RDA hierarchy but are wrong for a generic `dc:creator` value
   163Ôćĺwhere the creator role is unknown.
   164Ôćĺ
   165ÔćĺThe choice of WEMI level is determined by D9: since all ProvidedCHOs are typed
   166Ôćĺas `mocho:Manifestation`, the creator property should be at the Manifestation
   167Ôćĺlevel. `rdam:P30263` is present in mocho (confirmed in `mocho-full.owl`).
   168Ôćĺ
   169Ôćĺ**Note on alignment CSV**: `rdam:P30263` was found in `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`
   170Ôćĺunder `dc:format` with the wrong label "has reduction ratio designation" ÔÇö a
   171ÔćĺDCÔćĺRDA map derivation error (P30263 has no semantic relation to dc:format).
   172ÔćĺThe erroneous row was removed from both `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` and the
   173Ôćĺupstream `mocho/output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv`.
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ**Alternatives considered**:
   176Ôćĺ- *Emit all 464*: Semantically very noisy; a Goethe letter would assert
   177Ôćĺ  "has plaintiff corporate body" for the author. Rejected.
   178Ôćĺ- *Use rdaw:P10065 has creator agent of work*: Work-level; inconsistent with
   179Ôćĺ  the Manifestation typing of ProvidedCHO (D9). Rejected.
   180Ôćĺ- *Use mediatype dispatch*: Route to specific properties based on the record's
   181Ôćĺ  mediatype Concept IRI. Adds complexity; correct role remains unknown even
   182Ôćĺ  with mediatype. Rejected for POC.
   183Ôćĺ
   184Ôćĺ**Rationale**: `rdam:P30263` is the generic Manifestation-level creator property,
   185Ôćĺconsistent with the `mocho:Manifestation` base type assigned to all ProvidedCHOs
   186Ôćĺ(D9). Specific role properties should be emitted only after GND agent enrichment
   187Ôćĺ(Phase 1b) resolves the creator's function.
   188Ôćĺ
   189Ôćĺ---
   190Ôćĺ
   191Ôćĺ## Decision 8: Contributor fan-out ÔÇö keep dc:contributor as-is
   192Ôćĺ
   193Ôćĺ**Decision**: `dc:contributor` (json_key: `contributor`) is emitted using the
   194Ôćĺoriginal `dc:contributor` predicate
   195Ôćĺ(`http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/contributor`). No RDA property is used.
   196Ôćĺ
   197Ôćĺ**Background**: The alignment table produces 360 candidates for `contributor`,
   198Ôćĺspread across Expression (293), Manifestation (50), Item (16), and Work (1)
   199ÔćĺWEMI levels. The single Work-level candidate is "has academic supervisor" ÔÇö
   200Ôćĺsemantically wrong as a default. The Expression-level candidates are all specific
   201Ôćĺperformer roles (conductor, actor, dancer, etc.). No generic "has contributor
   202Ôćĺagent" superclass exists in mocho's current import.
   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ**Alternatives considered**:
   205Ôćĺ- *Whitelist generic RDA property*: No suitable candidate found. `P20052` in
   206Ôćĺ  the alignment table is incorrectly mapped as "has recordist agent" (a
   207Ôćĺ  derivation error in the DCÔćĺRDA map). Rejected.
   208Ôćĺ- *Emit all 360*: Semantically noisy; a 19th-century correspondence bundle
   209Ôćĺ  would assert "has dancer agent" for each addressee. Rejected.
   210Ôćĺ- *Skip contributor entirely*: Loses the contributor link for 26.2% of records
   211Ôćĺ  until Phase 1b. Rejected in favour of keeping the DC predicate.
   212Ôćĺ- *Mediatype dispatch*: Route to Manifestation-level properties by mediatype
   213Ôćĺ  (e.g. `mt003 Text Ôćĺ rdam:P30328 has contributor agent of text`). Adds
   214Ôćĺ  complexity and is still wrong for mixed-media records. Rejected for POC.
   215Ôćĺ
   216Ôćĺ**Rationale**: `dc:contributor` is a valid, well-understood predicate. Keeping
   217Ôćĺit preserves the contributor link in the output graph without asserting a
   218Ôćĺspecific role that cannot be determined from the DC value alone. The output is
   219Ôćĺnot mocho-RDA aligned for this field, but it is correct and queryable. Phase 1b
   220ÔćĺGND agent enrichment will replace this with typed RDA role triples.
   221Ôćĺ
   222Ôćĺ---
   223Ôćĺ
   224Ôćĺ## Consequences
   225Ôćĺ
   226Ôćĺ- `alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv` was patched: `dcTermsSubject` `edm_iri` corrected
   227Ôćĺ  from `dc:subject` to `dcterms:subject` (42 rows). If the alignment script is
   228Ôćĺ  re-run, this correction must be reapplied or the `OVERRIDES` dict in
   229Ôćĺ  `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py` must be extended to handle `dcTermsSubject`
   230Ôćĺ  explicitly.
   231Ôćĺ- Two fields (`creator`, `contributor`) bypass the alignment table entirely.
   232Ôćĺ  Their alignment table rows remain but are unused by the transform script; they
   233Ôćĺ  are still valid for downstream consumers (ISWC paper appendix, second-pass
   234Ôćĺ  scripts).
   235Ôćĺ- `dc:contributor` in the output is not mocho-RDA aligned. A Phase 1b script
   236Ôćĺ  (`link_gnd_agents.py`) will emit typed contributor triples via GND role lookup.
   237Ôćĺ- The transform stats (`transform_stats.json`) will show `contributor` and
   238Ôćĺ  `creator` with zero unmatched-key entries since they are handled before the
   239Ôćĺ  alignment lookup. Stats should record them under a separate `whitelisted_keys`
   240Ôćĺ  counter.
   241Ôćĺ- Every ProvidedCHO gets at least one rdf:type triple (`mocho:Manifestation`).
   242Ôćĺ  Objects with a mapped htype get two rdf:type triples. Objects with a pending
   243Ôćĺ  or absent htype get one. No `edm:ProvidedCHO` rdf:type triples appear in the
   244Ôćĺ  output; verification step 2 should grep for its absence.
   245Ôćĺ
   246Ôćĺ---
   247Ôćĺ
   248Ôćĺ## Decision 9: Every ProvidedCHO is typed as mocho:Manifestation
   249Ôćĺ
   250Ôćĺ**Decision**: `retype_cho()` unconditionally emits
   251Ôćĺ`<cho_uri> rdf:type mocho:Manifestation` for every ProvidedCHO. Where a
   252Ôćĺ`hierarchyType` code is present and maps to a DoCO or RiC-O class, that class
   253Ôćĺis emitted as an additional, more specific rdf:type. The `edm:ProvidedCHO`
   254Ôćĺfallback type is not emitted.
   255Ôćĺ
   256Ôćĺ**Rationale**: `edm:ProvidedCHO` is an EDM modelling artefact describing the
   257Ôćĺrole of the entity within an EDM Aggregation, not an ontological type for the
   258Ôćĺobject itself. In the mocho/WEMI model, DDB items ÔÇö regardless of media type,
   259Ôćĺinstitutional sector, or structural position ÔÇö represent cultural heritage
   260Ôćĺobjects as provided by an institution, which maps to the Manifestation level of
   261Ôćĺthe WEMI hierarchy. `mocho:Manifestation` is therefore the correct base type
   262Ôćĺfor all ProvidedCHOs.
   263Ôćĺ
   264ÔćĺThe DoCO or RiC-O class (where present) is a specialisation of this base type,
   265Ôćĺdescribing the structural or archival nature of the object within its
   266ÔćĺManifestation. Emitting both types allows SPARQL queries to match either the
   267Ôćĺgeneral Manifestation class or the specific structural class.
   268Ôćĺ
   269Ôćĺ**Alternatives considered**:
   270Ôćĺ- *Keep `edm:ProvidedCHO` as fallback*: Preserves EDM provenance but asserts
   271Ôćĺ  an EDM structural class in the mocho graph. Rejected: mixes EDM and mocho
   272Ôćĺ  semantics in a way that misleads downstream consumers.
   273Ôćĺ- *Omit rdf:type for pending/missing htype*: Leaves ~22,475 records (19.5%)
   274Ôćĺ  with no class. Rejected: untyped nodes are harder to query and do not reflect
   275Ôćĺ  the known WEMI alignment.
   276Ôćĺ- *Type as mocho:Work instead*: A DDB item could be argued to be a Work. Rejected:
   277Ôćĺ  the ProvidedCHO represents the object as held and provided ÔÇö the carrier level
   278Ôćĺ  ÔÇö which is Manifestation, not the abstract Work.
   279Ôćĺ
   280Ôćĺ---
   281Ôćĺ
   282Ôćĺ## Decision 10: PhysicalThing entities typed via htype lookup, no mocho:Manifestation
   283Ôćĺ
   284Ôćĺ**Decision**: `PhysicalThing` entities in the JSONL are archival hierarchy
   285Ôćĺancestors of the ProvidedCHO ÔÇö the finding aid lineage (Tektonik, Bestand,
   286ÔćĺGliederung, etc.) stored inline in each record. Each has its own `about` URI
   287Ôćĺand `hierarchyType`. They are typed via the same htype lookup as ProvidedCHO
   288Ôćĺbut receive **no** `mocho:Manifestation` base type. If `hierarchyType` is
   289Ôćĺabsent or pending, no rdf:type triple is emitted for that entity.
   290Ôćĺ
   291Ôćĺ**Background**: Corpus inspection of record `224BB273RJDT6WN7GAIRV4AJ5ES5YPC5`
   292Ôćĺconfirmed the structure. The PhysicalThing array carries the object's ancestor
   293Ôćĺnodes (e.g. two `htype_031 Gliederung` nodes). These are archival aggregation
   294Ôćĺcontainers ÔÇö `rico:RecordSet` with named individuals ÔÇö not carried objects.
   295ÔćĺThe DDB UI renders them as the "Verbundene Objekte" hierarchy tree.
   296Ôćĺ
   297Ôćĺ**Rationale**: `mocho:Manifestation` is the base type for carried objects
   298Ôćĺ(ProvidedCHO, D9). Archival hierarchy nodes are `rico:RecordSet` / `rico:Record`
   299Ôćĺ/ `rico:RecordPart` depending on htype ÔÇö a different branch of the mocho type
   300Ôćĺsystem. Applying `mocho:Manifestation` to them would conflate the carried object
   301Ôćĺwith its archival containers and produce incorrect SPARQL results.
   302Ôćĺ
   303Ôćĺ**Implementation**: `retype_cho()` is renamed to `retype_entities()` and
   304Ôćĺextended to iterate over both `ProvidedCHO` (single dict) and `PhysicalThing`
   305Ôćĺ(array). ProvidedCHO logic unchanged (D9). PhysicalThing loop: htype lookup Ôćĺ
   306Ôćĺemit RiC-O class and `rico:hasRecordSetType` where applicable; no Manifestation
   307Ôćĺassertion; no fallback.
   308Ôćĺ



```

    21Ôćĺ

    24Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>Authored by Margaret Warren and Pat Hayes for Metadata Authoring Systems, LLC, Metadata Authoring Technologies, LLC and ImageSnippets(tm) 2010</rdfs:comment>

    26Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>LIO</rdfs:label>

    28Ôćĺ    <vann:preferredNamespaceUri>https://w3id.org/lio/v1#</vann:preferredNamespaceUri>
    29Ôćĺ    <dcterms:title>Lightweight Image Ontology (LIO)</dcterms:title>
    30Ôćĺ    <dcterms:description>A Lightweight Ontology for Describing Images</dcterms:description>
    31Ôćĺ    <dcterms:license rdf:resource="https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/"/>
    32Ôćĺ  </owl:Ontology>
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ  <owl:Class rdf:ID="PictorialElement">
    35Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentClass rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#PictorialElement"/>
    36Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>Pictorial element</rdfs:label>
    37Ôćĺ    <rdfs:isDefinedBy rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/lio/v1"/>
    38Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment> Any visual entity; any graphical item, part, or visual property of an image or part of an image, including the image itself. Examples include lines, areas, colors and textures. Pictorial elements may have arbitrarily complex shapes and may be implicit, e.g. lines of perspective or outlined white spaces. Rectangular regions of an image are pictorial elements, and the entire image is also. Thus, Image is a subclass of PictorialElement. PictorialElements may be included in one another, giving a simple mereology of visual forms, with the property visualPartOf. Non-visual properties such as date or manner of creation are not considered to be PictorialElements. A property or aspect of an image can be considered to be a PictorialElment when it can be apprehended by vision alone.</rdfs:comment>
    39Ôćĺ  </owl:Class>
    40Ôćĺ
    41Ôćĺ  <owl:Class rdf:ID="Image">
    42Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentClass rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#Image"/>
    43Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>Image</rdfs:label>
    44Ôćĺ    <rdfs:isDefinedBy rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/lio/v1"/>
    45Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>An image. Any PictorialElement that is considered to be a complete image. Typically the main subject of an ImageSnippets description will be in this class. An image can be a visualPartOf another image.</rdfs:comment>
    46Ôćĺ    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>
    47Ôćĺ  </owl:Class>
    48Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="visualPartOf">

    53Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>
    54Ôćĺ    <rdfs:range rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>

    58Ôćĺ
    59Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="hasVisualPart">
    60Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentProperty rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#hasVisualPart"/>
    61Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>has visual part</rdfs:label>
    62Ôćĺ    <rdfs:isDefinedBy rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/lio/v1"/>
    63Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>The inverse of the part-of relation on visual elements. </rdfs:comment>
    64Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>
    65Ôćĺ    <rdfs:range rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>
    66Ôćĺ    <owl:inverseOf rdf:resource="#visualPartOf"/>
    67Ôćĺ    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#TransitiveProperty"/>
    68Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
    69Ôćĺ   
    70Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="depicts">
    71Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentProperty rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#depicts"/>
    72Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>depicts</rdfs:label>
    73Ôćĺ    <rdfs:isDefinedBy rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/lio/v1"/>
    74Ôćĺ    <rdfs:subPropertyOf rdf:resource="#shows"/>
    75Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>
    76Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>Anything depicted by an image. Subproperty of lio:shows. Depiction does not imply looking like, eg an Xray of a chest can be said to depict the patient. As an intuitive guide, an image depicts anything it can be said to be a picture of. </rdfs:comment>
    77Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
    78Ôćĺ  
    79Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="shows">
    80Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentProperty rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#shows"/>
    81Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>shows</rdfs:label>
    82Ôćĺ    <rdfs:isDefinedBy rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/lio/v1"/>
    83Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#Image"/>
    84Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment> Anything in the scene depicted by an image, and which is visible in the image. Meant to include incidental depictions as well as any main subjects. For example, a tree in the background of a portrait, or a person in the crowd at a parade. Images may show many things that the images are not a picture of. </rdfs:comment>
    85Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
    86Ôćĺ    
    87Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="looksLike">
    88Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentProperty rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#looksLike"/>
    89Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>looks like</rdfs:label>
    90Ôćĺ    <rdfs:isDefinedBy rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/lio/v1"/>
    91Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>
    92Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>Characterization of something in terms of what it resembles or appears to be a representation of.  Examples include 'an outline of a man', a hand shape, etc.. Note, this property has a special meaning when applied to classes. When the value is a class or skos:category, this means that the visual element is a 'typical' or archetypical or iconic shape for an item in that class, such as a typical elephant outline. When the value is an individual, this means that the element visually resembles the individual, eg a profile of George Washington.
    93Ôćĺ    </rdfs:comment>
    94Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="conveys">
    97Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentProperty rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#conveys"/>
    98Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>conveys</rdfs:label>
    99Ôćĺ    <rdfs:isDefinedBy rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/lio/v1"/>
   100Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>Relationship between an image and an emotion, mood or idea that it can be said to illustrate without actually depicting. FOr example, a Picasso blue period painting might convey sadness.</rdfs:comment>
   101Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>
   102Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
   103Ôćĺ
   104Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="usesPictorially">
   105Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentProperty rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#usesPictorially"/>
   106Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>uses pictorially</rdfs:label>
   107Ôćĺ    <rdfs:isDefinedBy rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/lio/v1"/>
   108Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>Relationship between an image and something visible (depicted or shown) in the image when that thing is used for pictorial or artistic effect. </rdfs:comment>
   109Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>
   110Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
   111Ôćĺ

   114Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>has artistic element</rdfs:label>
   115Ôćĺ    <rdfs:isDefinedBy rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/lio/v1"/>
   116Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>Relationship between an image and a pictorial element in the image (a line, shape, form, texture) which is used for pictorial or artistic effect. </rdfs:comment>
   117Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>
   118Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="hasInForeground">

   124Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>Relationship between an image and something visible in the foreground of the scene depicted by the image. </rdfs:comment>
   125Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#PictorialElement"/>
   126Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
   127Ôćĺ
   128Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="hasInBackground">
   129Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentProperty rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#hasInBackground"/>
   130Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>has in background</rdfs:label>

   132Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>Relationship between an image and something visible in the background of the scene depicted by the image.</rdfs:comment>

   134Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="hasDepictedBackground">
   137Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentProperty rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#hasDepictedBackground"/>
   138Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>has depicted background</rdfs:label>
   139Ôćĺ    <rdfs:isDefinedBy rdf:resource="https://w3id.org/lio/v1"/>
   140Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#Image"/>
   141Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>Relation between an image and the background of the scene depicted by the image, for example distant mountains. Not all images need have such a background. As with looksLike, when the value is a class or skos:Concept, this means a typical element of that class, eg http://dbpedia.org/resource/Mountain. </rdfs:comment>
   142Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
   143Ôćĺ  
   144Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="hasPictorialBackground">
   145Ôćĺ    <owl:equivalentProperty rdf:resource="http://purl.org/net/lio#hasPictorialBackground"/>
   146Ôćĺ    <rdfs:label>has pictorial background</rdfs:label>

   148Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#Image"/>
   149Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>The pictorial (surface) background of the image itself. For example, the ground surface of a drawing. Not all images need have such a background. As with looksLike, when the value is a class or skos:Concept, this means a typical element of that class. </rdfs:comment>
   150Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
   151Ôćĺ  
   152Ôćĺ  <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:ID="hasSetting">

   156Ôćĺ    <rdfs:domain rdf:resource="#Image"/>
   157Ôćĺ    <rdfs:comment>Circumstances or general location which provide a context within which the image was created. This may be a location or an event, such as http://dbpedia.org/resource/Millennium_March_on_Washington. It need not be depicted in the image explicitly. As with looksLike, when the value is a class or skos:concept, this is understood to mean a typical instance of the class, eg a photograph taken in a kitchen hasSetting http://http://dbpedia.org/resource/Kitchen. </rdfs:comment>
   158Ôćĺ  </owl:ObjectProperty>
   159Ôćĺ

### Assistant *(2026-04-17T09:42:35.478Z)*

Major finding. In mocho-edit.owl:
- `mocho:Manifestation` union **includes `vra:Image`** ÔÇö correct for visual CHOs
- `mocho:Work` union includes **`vra:Work`** and **`lio:Image`** ÔÇö NOT appropriate for ProvidedCHOs typed as mocho:Manifestation
- No DisjointClasses, but using `vra:Work`/`lio:Image` on a Manifestation-typed CHO contradicts the mocho design intent

Also: VRA Core 4.0 OWL defines both `vra:Work` and `vra:Image` as simple subclasses of `schema:CreativeWork` ÔÇö no hierarchy between them, only linked by `vra:imageOf` property.

This changes the plan significantly. Let me rewrite ┬ž1.

---

### Assistant *(2026-04-17T09:43:08.225Z)*

**Tool call:** `Edit`
```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/.claude/plans/wobbly-swimming-moon.md",
  "old_string": "## 1. VRA Core + LIO conceptual mapping\n\n**VRA Work vs Image** (rm023-vra.pdf):\n- `vra:Work` ÔÇö the physical/conceptual artwork (drawing, painting, print). Central class.\n  Properties: creator, material, technique, stylePeriod, culturalContext, dateCreated.\n- `vra:Image` ÔÇö the image documentation of a work. Connected to `vra:Work` via `vra:imageOf`.\n- These are separate classes; a DDB CHO is one or the other, not both.\n\n**LIO three-layer model** (rm024-lio.pdf, Hayes & Warren 2010):\n- Layer 1: **object** (physical artifact ÔÇö furniture, person, artwork)\n- Layer 2: **photo of the object** (the CHO for photographic museum records)\n- Layer 3: **scan of the photo** (the `edm:WebResource` / `edm:isShownBy` target)\n- `foaf:Image` = the subject image (the digital scan, layer 3) ÔÇö this is NOT the ProvidedCHO\n- `lio:fascimile` = \"sameImageAs\", relates layer 3 to layer 2\n- `lio:depicts` = what the image is about (links image to subject terms, people, events)\n\n**Critical insight**: `lio:Image` (= `foaf:Image` in mocho-edit.owl) applies to the\n*digital representation*, not necessarily to the ProvidedCHO. However, mocho uses\n`lio:Image` alongside `vra:Image` for museum photographic records because the CHO\nis itself the digitised photo (layers 2 and 3 collapse in the DDB metadata model).\n\n---",
  "new_string": "## 1. VRA Core + LIO conceptual mapping\n\n### 1.1 VRA Core 4.0 class structure\n\nFrom `mocho/ontology/vra-core-v4.0.owl` (IRI base `http://purl.org/vra/`):\n\n- `vra:Work` ÔÇö `rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork`. The artwork as a conceptual/creative entity.\n  Carries properties: creator, material, technique, stylePeriod, culturalContext, dateCreated, about.\n- `vra:Image` ÔÇö `rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork`. A visual image entity.\n  No OWL hierarchy between `vra:Work` and `vra:Image` ÔÇö they are sibling classes under `schema:CreativeWork`.\n- `vra:Collection` ÔÇö `rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork`.\n- `vra:imageOf` ÔÇö ObjectProperty; `owl:inverseOf vra:hasImage`. Links a `vra:Image` to the `vra:Work` it documents.\n- No `rdfs:domain` or `rdfs:range` constraints on `vra:imageOf` in the OWL file.\n- **No DisjointClasses axioms** between `vra:Work` and `vra:Image`.\n\nFrom rm023-vra.pdf: `vra:Work` is the \"central class\" in VRA for cultural heritage objects.\n`vra:Image` is a separate documentation image of a work, linked via `vra:imageOf`.\n\n### 1.2 LIO class and property structure\n\nFrom `mocho/odk-ontology/lio_import.owl` (IRI base `https://w3id.org/lio/v1#`):\n\n- `lio:Image` ÔÇö subclass of `lio:PictorialElement`. \"Any PictorialElement that is considered to\n  be a complete image.\" Not restricted to photographs ÔÇö covers drawings, paintings, etc.\n- `lio:PictorialElement` ÔÇö \"Any visual entity; any graphical item, part, or visual property of an image.\"\n- `lio:depicts` ÔÇö domain: PictorialElement. What the image is a picture of (broad sense: people,\n  places, events, iconography, function/purpose). Subproperty of `lio:shows`.\n- `lio:shows` ÔÇö domain: Image. Incidental visible elements (background tree, crowd member).\n- **No `lio:fascimile` property** in this OWL file. The Hayes & Warren paper discusses\n  `mw:facsimile` (using namespace `mw:`), which is NOT the same as `lio:fascimile`. The property\n  does not exist in the mocho-imported LIO OWL. **Correction to previous plan**: `lio:fascimile`\n  was a misconception ÔÇö the property is `mw:facsimile` (a different namespace) and is not in mocho.\n\n### 1.3 Where VRA and LIO sit in the mocho WEMI hierarchy\n\nFrom `mocho-edit.owl` union axioms (confirmed):\n\n```\nmocho:Manifestation ÔŐć ObjectUnionOf(\n  mo:MusicalManifestation, mo:Record,\n  vra:Image,                ÔćÉ HERE\n  rdac:C10007, ec:MediaResource, aco:AudioManifestation, rico:Instantiation\n)\n\nmocho:Work ÔŐć ObjectUnionOf(\n  mo:MusicalWork, frbr:Work, vra:Collection, vra:Work,   ÔćÉ vra:Work HERE\n  rdac:C10001, crm:E89, ec:EditorialWork,\n  lio:Image,                ÔćÉ lio:Image HERE (not Manifestation!)\n  rico:Record, rico:RecordResource, rico:RecordSet\n)\n```\n\n**Key consequences**:\n\n1. `vra:Image` is in the **Manifestation** union ÔÇö consistent with D9 (ProvidedCHO = Manifestation).\n   Use `vra:Image` for visual ProvidedCHOs.\n\n2. `lio:Image` is in the **Work** union ÔÇö NOT the Manifestation union. Emitting `lio:Image` for a\n   ProvidedCHO typed as `mocho:Manifestation` goes against mocho's design intent. There are no\n   DisjointClasses axioms so it's not formally inconsistent, but it is semantically wrong.\n\n3. `vra:Work` is in the **Work** union ÔÇö same problem. DDB ProvidedCHOs are Manifestation-level\n   entities (D9). Using `vra:Work` for a ProvidedCHO conflates the physical carrier (Manifestation)\n   with the abstract creative work (Work).\n\n**No DisjointClasses** between `mocho:Work` and `mocho:Manifestation` exist in the OWL, so these\nare not formal contradictions, but the mocho design is clear: `vra:Image` Ôćĺ Manifestation; `vra:Work`\nand `lio:Image` Ôćĺ Work.\n\n### 1.4 The three-layer model and DDB\n\nFrom rm024-lio.pdf (\"Semantics of Photographs\"):\n- Layer 1: **object** (the thing photographed ÔÇö furniture, artifact)\n- Layer 2: **photo of the object** (the photographic artifact in the museum collection)\n- Layer 3: **scan of the photo** (the `edm:WebResource` / `edm:isShownBy` target)\n\nIn DDB, the ProvidedCHO is typically **layer 2** for photographic records: the photo AS a museum\nobject. The digital scan (layer 3) is accessible via `edm:isShownBy` or `edm:hasView` links.\n\nFor artwork records (Zeichnung, Gem├Ąlde): the ProvidedCHO IS the artwork ÔÇö it collapses layers 1\nand 2 (the physical drawing is both the artwork and what is described). In VRA, the drawing would be\n`vra:Work`. In mocho, however, the ProvidedCHO is always typed as `mocho:Manifestation`, so the VRA\nWork-level identity is NOT expressed via rdf:type on the ProvidedCHO.\n\n---"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-04-17T09:43:35.514Z)*

**Tool call:** `Edit`
```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/.claude/plans/wobbly-swimming-moon.md",
  "old_string": "## 2. dc:type taxonomy for mt002\n\n### 2.1 Photographic types Ôćĺ `vra:Image` (+ `lio:Image` for sparte005)\n\nThe DDB record describes a photographic artifact. The CHO is the photograph, negative,\nslide, or scan.\n\n```\nFotografie, Foto, Standfoto, Kontaktbogen, Kleinbilddia, Bilddokument,\nFotomechanische Reproduktion, Fotoalbum (as a collection of photos),\nPostkarte (photographic postcard)\n```\n\nType assignment:\n- `rdf_type_m = vra:Image` (IRI: `http://purl.org/vra/Image`)\n- For sparte005 (Museum): also emit `lio:Image` (IRI: `https://w3id.org/lio/v1#Image`)\n- For sparte001 (Archive): also emit `rico:Record` (archival photograph)\n\n### 2.2 Artwork types Ôćĺ `vra:Work`\n\nThe DDB record describes a physical visual artwork. The digital image is documentation\nof the work, not the work itself.\n\n```\nZeichnung, Druckgraphik, Druckgrafik, Druckschrift, Gem├Ąlde, Malerei,\nGrafik, Graphik, Kunst, Wandbild, Druck (in sparte006 context),\nMappenwerk, Plakat (as designed artwork), Geldschein, Medaille\n```\n\nType assignment:\n- `rdf_type_m = vra:Work` (IRI: `http://purl.org/vra/Work`)\n- No `lio:Image` ÔÇö the CHO is not an image, it is an object\n\n### 2.3 Ambiguous types ÔÇö sector-dependent\n\n| dc:type | sparte005 | sparte006 |\n|---|---|---|\n| Druck | `vra:Image` (photographic print) | `vra:Work` (printed artwork) |\n| Album Zeichnung | `vra:Work` (collection of drawings) | `vra:Work` |\n| Plakat | `vra:Work` (designed poster) | `vra:Work` |\n\n---",
  "new_string": "## 2. rdf:type dispatch for mt002\n\n**Single rule**: all mt002 ProvidedCHOs (regardless of dc:type) Ôćĺ `vra:Image`.\n\nRationale:\n- `vra:Image` is in the `mocho:Manifestation` union (┬ž1.3) ÔÇö the only VRA class appropriate\n  for Manifestation-typed ProvidedCHOs.\n- `vra:Work` is in the `mocho:Work` union ÔÇö would imply the CHO is a Work, contradicting D9.\n- `lio:Image` is in the `mocho:Work` union ÔÇö same problem.\n- `vra:Image` semantically covers the photographic and visual-artifact case: both a photograph\n  and a drawing are visual images as far as mocho:Manifestation is concerned.\n\n### 2.1 The artwork/photo distinction is NOT expressed via rdf:type on the ProvidedCHO\n\nThe difference between a photographic artifact (Fotografie) and a physical artwork (Zeichnung)\nis captured through:\n1. **dc:type literal** ÔÇö preserved via property alignment (already done; `dc:type` Ôćĺ RDA property)\n2. **`vra:imageOf` property** (future work) ÔÇö would link a photographic ProvidedCHO to the\n   `vra:Work` entity it documents. Requires separate Work-level entities to be created. Out of scope\n   for this pass.\n3. **`lio:depicts`** (future work) ÔÇö links the image to its subject terms (people, places, events)\n\n### 2.2 Archive sector (sparte001) + mt002\n\nFor archival photographic records, `rico:Record` may co-occur with `vra:Image`. The htype dispatch\nalready handles `rico:Record` for archival hierarchy types. The dc:type dispatch layer adds `vra:Image`\nindependently. Both are emitted and deduped per the accumulate model (┬ž6).\n\nException: if dc:type = \"Brief\", \"Handschrift\", \"Abschrift\" etc. (non-visual archival types in mt002\nÔÇö these are misclassified under mt002 in the corpus), the dc:type lookup emits `vra:Image` anyway\nbecause the mediatype gate is mt002. These dc:types are better handled at the property alignment\nlayer (already done).\n\n### 2.3 Lookup table entry\n\nAll mt002 entries in `lookup_dctype_to_class.csv`:\n- `rdf_type_m = http://purl.org/vra/Image`\n- `source_vocab = vra`\n\nNo `lio:Image` rows in the lookup. No `vra:Work` rows.\n\n---"
}

Let me generate the summary based on what I've found:

## Search Results: RDA Classes and Properties for Digital/Online Resources

Based on my systematic search of the mocho RDA ontology files, here are the key findings:

### 1. **Carrier Type Property (rdam.owl)**
**File:** `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/mocho-odk/mocho/mirror/rdam.owl`

- **Property IRI:** `http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30001`
- **Label:** "has carrier type"
- **Toolkit Label:** "carrier type"
- **Definition:** "A categorization reflecting the format of the storage medium and housing of a carrier in combination with the type of intermediation device required to view, play, run, or otherwise access the content of a manifestation."
- **Lexical Alias:** `http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/carrierType.en`

### 2. **Digital Encoding Schema Property (rdam.owl)**
**File:** `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/mocho-odk/mocho/mirror/rdam.owl`

- **Property IRI:** `http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30096`
- **Definition:** "A schema or standard that is used to encode the digital content of a manifestation."
- **Toolkit Definition:** "A schema or standard that is used to encode the digital content of a manifestation."
- **Instruction Number:** 3.19.3

### 3. **Online Resource Carrier Type (rdact.csv - RDA Carrier Type Vocabulary)**
**File:** `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/ontology/rda-5.4.9/csv/termList/rdact.csv` (line 37)

- **Preferred Label:** "online resource"
- **IRI:** `http://rdaregistry.info/termList/RDAct/1018`
- **Definition:** "A carrier type that consists of a digital resource accessed by means of hardware and software connections to a communications network."
- **Notation:** 1018
- **Status:** Published

### 4. **Content Type: Computer Dataset and Computer Program (rdaco.csv)**
**File:** `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/ontology/rda-5.4.9/csv/termList/rdaco.csv`

For digitally-encoded content types:
- **Computer dataset** (rdaco:1007): "A content type that consists of content expressed through a digitally encoded dataset intended to be processed by a computer."
- **Computer program** (rdaco:1008): "A content type that consists of content expressed through digitally encoded instructions intended to be processed and performed by a computer."
- **Cartographic dataset** (rdaco:1001): "A content type that consists of cartographic content expressed through a digitally encoded dataset intended to be processed by a computer."

### 5. **mocho-edit.owl Status**
**File:** `/Users/mta/Documents/claude/mocho/mocho-odk/mocho/mocho-edit.owl`

- **Manifestation class:** `<https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#Manifestation>` (line 70)
- No mocho-specific subclass for "digital manifestation" exists yet; the ontology imports RDA classes via `rdam_import.owl` (line 29)

### Summary

**For representing a "digital manifestation" or "online resource" in RDA:**

1. Use the **carrier type** `http://rdaregistry.info/termList/RDAct/1018` ("online resource") via property `http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30001`
2. Optionally specify the **content type** using values from `rdaco` (e.g., computer dataset, computer program)
3. Optionally specify the **encoding schema** via `http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/P30096` (e.g., JSON, XML, etc.)

There is **no dedicated RDA class** for "digital manifestation" ÔÇö RDA uses the generic `rdac:C10007` (Manifestation) with the carrier type property to indicate it's an online resource.

### Assistant *(2026-04-17T11:31:52.893Z)*

**On WebResource (digital manifestation):** RDA has no dedicated class. The pattern is `rdac:C10007` (Manifestation) + property `rdam:P30001 has carrier type` with value `http://rdaregistry.info/termList/RDAct/1018` ("online resource"). So the WebResource typing would be:

- `mocho:Manifestation` + `rdac:C10007` (via property: `rdam:P30001 rdact:1018`) + `vra:Image`

**On archival photos (point 3):** Confirmed ÔÇö accumulate both:

| Entity | Types | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| ProvidedCHO (archive photo) | `rico:Record` + `lio:Image` | It IS an archival record AND a photograph as a creative work |
| WebResource | `mocho:Manifestation` + `rdac:C10007` + `vra:Image` | Digital scan = online manifestation |

So `rico:Record` comes from the htype/archival dispatch (existing), and `lio:Image` is added by the dc:type IMAGE dispatch ÔÇö both layer onto the same CHO. The accumulate+dedup model handles this cleanly.

**Full picture so far:**

| CHO type | ProvidedCHO rdf:type | WebResource rdf:type |
|---|---|---|
| Museum artwork (Zeichnung, Gem├Ąlde) | `vra:Work` | `mocho:Manifestation` + `rdac:C10007` + `vra:Image` |
| Museum photo (Fotografie) | `lio:Image` + `mocho:Manifestation` | `mocho:Manifestation` + `rdac:C10007` + `vra:Image` |
| Archive photo (sparte001 + mt002) | `rico:Record` + `lio:Image` | `mocho:Manifestation` + `rdac:C10007` + `vra:Image` |
| Library/Archive non-image | `mocho:Manifestation` (D9 unchanged) | ÔÇö |

Two remaining open points:
- **sparte006 (Performing Arts) + mt002**: same as museum (sparte005) ÔÇö artworks Ôćĺ `vra:Work`, photos Ôćĺ `lio:Image`?
- **Fotografie ProvidedCHO**: you said `lio:Image` + `mocho:Manifestation` (both) ÔÇö confirming it spans both WEMI levels, consistent with "image IS its own work"?
