### Assistant *(2026-04-17T14:20:50.625Z)*

**Tool call:** `ExitPlanMode`
```json
{
  "plan": "# Plan: Map DDB IMAGE Objects to mocho ÔÇö VRA Core + mocho:ImageWork / mocho:ImageObject\n\n## 0. Context\n\nDDB records with `edm:type = \"IMAGE\"` and `mediatype = mt002` span ontologically\ndistinct categories. The existing approach (D9: every ProvidedCHO = `mocho:Manifestation`)\nis incorrect for museum visual objects.\n\nRevised model ÔÇö two new native mocho classes:\n\n| Entity | Class | WEMI level |\n|---|---|---|\n| Artwork ProvidedCHO (Zeichnung, Gem├Ąlde) | `vra:Work` | Work |\n| Photo ProvidedCHO (Fotografie, Standfoto) | `mocho:ImageWork` | Work |\n| WebResource (digital scan / image file) | `mocho:ImageObject` | Manifestation |\n\n`vra:Image` is aligned to Manifestation in the babel-ddb paper (\"visual surrogate or\ndigital reproduction\"); `vra:Image rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork` in the OWL\n(WEMI-neutral), but the semantic intent is documentation image, not photo-as-Work.\nTherefore `vra:Image` is NOT used for ProvidedCHOs. `mocho:ImageObject` subsumes its\nManifestation role at the WebResource level.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Conceptual framework\n\n### 1.1 VRA Core 4.0 classes (from `mocho/ontology/vra-core-v4.0.owl`)\n\nIRI base: `http://purl.org/vra/`\n\n- **`vra:Work`** ÔÇö `rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork`. The artwork as a creative/physical entity.\n  2D artworks (Zeichnung, Gem├Ąlde), 3D sculptures, architecture.\n- **`vra:Image`** ÔÇö `rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork`. A visual image artifact (photograph,\n  slide, documentation image). **Work level** ÔÇö see ┬ž1.1.1 for the full discussion.\n- **`vra:imageOf`** ÔÇö ObjectProperty, `owl:inverseOf vra:hasImage`. Links a `vra:Image` to\n  the `vra:Work` it documents.\n\n#### 1.1.1 Discussion: WEMI level of `vra:Image`\n\n**The babel-ddb alignment (now revised)**\n\nThe babel-ddb paper placed `vra:Image` at **Manifestation** level using a structural argument:\nVRA Core's tripartite schema (Work Ôćĺ Image, no intermediate Expression) collapses the FRBR\nWÔćĺEÔćĺM hierarchy, so Image occupies the slot where Manifestation would be. Under this reading,\n`vra:Image` is a visual surrogate or digital reproduction of a `vra:Work` ÔÇö a carrier, not\na creative entity.\n\n**The problem: `schema:CreativeWork` is not WEMI-aligned**\n\n`vra:Image rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork`. Schema.org has no WEMI hierarchy: its\n`schema:CreativeWork` covers books, paintings, photographs, recordings, and software ÔÇö entities\nthat span all FRBR levels. Schema.org classes are **WEMI-neutral by design**, but they carry\n**WEMI implications through naming and semantic intent**: \"CreativeWork\" reads as a creative\nor intellectual entity, which is Work-level intuition. A reasoner that maps\n`schema:CreativeWork Ôćĺ mocho:Work` would infer `vra:Image ÔŐĹ mocho:Work`, directly\ncontradicting the babel-ddb Manifestation placement.\n\n**Hayes & Warren** compound the ambiguity: *\"In many cases the subject image itself will be\nthe work.\"* A photograph is a creative act; the photographer made deliberate choices. From\nthis angle, a `vra:Image` (a photograph) is unambiguously a Work.\n\n**VRA Core's own design intent** supports the Work reading too: VRA describes visual arts\ncollections from the perspective of image creators. `vra:Work` is the collected artifact;\n`vra:Image` is the visual record of it ÔÇö but the photograph of an artwork is itself an\nartifact created by someone, not merely a carrier. VRA does not call `vra:Image` a\nManifestation anywhere in its specification.\n\n**Conclusion and revised alignment**\n\n`vra:Image` is treated as **Work level** in this plan, consistent with:\n- Its OWL superclass `schema:CreativeWork`\n- VRA Core's creator-centric framing\n- Hayes & Warren's observation that images are often Works in their own right\n\nThe babel-ddb structural argument (Manifestation slot in a collapsed hierarchy) is noted\nbut not adopted here. mocho follows the model, not the babel-ddb paper. The key implication:\n`vra:Image` belongs in the `mocho:Work` union (not Manifestation); WebResources are typed\nwith `mocho:ImageObject` (`rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Manifestation`) to make the carrier level\nexplicit without relying on `vra:Image`.\n\n### 1.2 LIO (from `mocho/odk-ontology/lio_import.owl`)\n\nIRI base: `https://w3id.org/lio/v1#`\n\n- **`lio:depicts`**, **`lio:shows`** ÔÇö kept for property-level alignment; not used for rdf:type dispatch.\n- **`lio:Image`** ÔÇö NOT used for rdf:type dispatch (see ┬ž1.2.1).\n- **`mw:facsimile`** ÔÇö Hayes & Warren 2010 (`http://purl.org/net/mw/`).\n  `facsimileOf (Image, Image)`: the subject is a reproduction of the object.\n  `lio:fascimile` appeared in rm024-lio.pdf slides but was never implemented in the LIO OWL.\n  **Resolution**: define `mocho:facsimile` natively (see ┬ž1.3.3). Follow-up ADR.\n\n#### 1.2.1 Decision: `lio:Image` vs `mocho:ImageWork` for photo ProvidedCHOs\n\n**Pros of `lio:Image`**\n- Already imported in mocho (`lio_import.owl`) ÔÇö no new class definition required\n- Designed specifically for image description; semantically appropriate for photographs\n- WEMI-neutral in the LIO OWL ÔÇö does not over-commit to Work or Manifestation\n- Already in the mocho:Work union (prior state of mocho-edit.owl)\n- Broad definition: \"any PictorialElement considered to be a complete image\" ÔÇö covers photos, drawings, paintings\n\n**Cons of `lio:Image`**\n- Research ontology; not widely adopted in production CH linked data pipelines\n- WEMI-neutrality is also a weakness: the class gives no signal about level; dispatch logic must always pair it with `mocho:Work` or `mocho:Manifestation` at instance level\n- Semantically overlaps with `vra:Image` and `foaf:Image`; unclear precedence\n- `lio:fascimile` was planned in LIO but never implemented ÔÇö the OWL is incomplete, raising confidence concerns about the namespace long-term\n- Mixes an image-description ontology (LIO) with CH-domain ontologies (VRA, RDA, RiC-O) in the same dispatch layer\n\n**Note on `vra:Image` as Manifestation**\n\nThe babel-ddb paper aligns `vra:Image` to Manifestation level (structural argument: VRA collapses\nWÔćĺEÔćĺM to WÔćĺImage, so Image occupies the Manifestation slot). However, `vra:Image rdfs:subClassOf\nschema:CreativeWork` in the OWL ÔÇö and `schema:CreativeWork` reads as a Work-level entity by name\nand Schema.org intent (though Schema.org has no WEMI at all). A reasoner that maps\n`schema:CreativeWork Ôćĺ mocho:Work` would infer `vra:Image ÔŐĹ mocho:Work`, directly contradicting\nthe babel-ddb Manifestation placement. `vra:Image` is therefore ontologically unreliable for\nexplicit WEMI dispatch in mocho: its OWL superclass pulls toward Work; its VRA design intent\nand babel-ddb alignment pull toward Manifestation.\n\n**Final decision: `mocho:ImageWork`**\n\nReasons:\n- `rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work` gives explicit, unambiguous WEMI commitment ÔÇö no contradiction from inherited superclasses\n- Pairs symmetrically with `mocho:ImageObject` (`rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Manifestation`) for WebResources ÔÇö both have mocho-native, clean WEMI chains\n- Avoids the `vra:Image` / `schema:CreativeWork` WEMI ambiguity entirely\n- Native mocho class ÔÇö no external LIO dependency required for dispatch; LIO import retained only for property use (`lio:depicts`, `lio:shows`)\n- Name is self-documenting in the mocho namespace; aligns with mocho's role as a hub ontology\n\n### 1.3 New mocho classes\n\n#### 1.3.1 `mocho:ImageWork` ÔÇö photographic Work\n\nPhoto ProvidedCHOs: the photograph is the creative artifact collected and described in DDB.\n\n```turtle\nmocho:ImageWork rdf:type owl:Class ;\n    rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work ;\n    rdfs:label \"Image Work\"@en ;\n    rdfs:comment \"\"\"A photographic or visual image artifact treated as a Work-level entity:\n        the photograph, slide, negative, or postcard is itself the creative artifact\n        collected and described. Distinct from vra:Image (documentation image of an\n        artwork, Manifestation level).\"\"\"@en .\n```\n\n#### 1.3.2 `mocho:ImageObject` ÔÇö digital image carrier (Manifestation)\n\nWebResources: the digital file that carries a Work (artwork or photo).\n\n```turtle\nmocho:ImageObject rdf:type owl:Class ;\n    rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Manifestation ;\n    rdfs:label \"Image Object\"@en ;\n    rdfs:comment \"\"\"A digital image carrier at Manifestation level: a scan, online image\n        file, or digital reproduction. Used exclusively for WebResources (edm:isShownBy /\n        edm:hasView targets). Subsumes the vra:Image role (visual surrogate /\n        documentation image).\"\"\"@en .\n```\n\n#### 1.3.3 `mocho:facsimile` ÔÇö reproduction link (follow-up ADR)\n\nSource definition ÔÇö Hayes & Warren 2010, p. 74, Properties (Domain, Range):\n\n> ***facsimileOf*** (Image, Image) ÔÇö \"The subject is a more or less precise re-rendering,\n> reproduction or visual representation of the object. A photograph of a drawing taken for\n> exhibition in a catalog would be the typical example. It does not imply exactness of\n> reproduction: a lower-resolution thumbnail can be a facsimile, for example.\"\n\n```turtle\nmocho:facsimile rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty ;\n    rdfs:label \"facsimile of\"@en ;\n    rdfs:comment \"\"\"The subject is a more or less precise re-rendering, reproduction or\n        visual representation of the object. A photograph of a drawing taken for exhibition\n        in a catalog would be the typical example. It does not imply exactness of\n        reproduction: a lower-resolution thumbnail can be a facsimile, for example.\n        (After Hayes & Warren 2010 mw:facsimileOf, p. 74.)\"\"\"@en ;\n    rdfs:domain mocho:ImageObject ;\n    rdfs:range  owl:Thing .   # mocho:ImageWork or vra:Work\n```\n\n### 1.4 mocho-edit.owl changes required\n\n**Summary of changes**\n\n| Change | Detail |\n|---|---|\n| Define `mocho:ImmovableWork` | New OWL class, `rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work` |\n| Define `mocho:ImageWork` | New OWL class, `rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work` |\n| Define `mocho:ImageObject` | New OWL class, `rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Manifestation` |\n| `mocho:Work` union | Add `mocho:ImageWork`; add `vra:Image` (Work level, revised); remove `lio:Image` |\n| `mocho:Manifestation` union | Add `mocho:ImageObject`; remove `vra:Image` (no longer Manifestation) |\n| Define `mocho:facsimile` | **Out of scope** ÔÇö not used in goethe-faust corpus; follow-up ADR only |\n\n**OWL definitions to add to `mocho-edit.owl`**\n\n```turtle\n@prefix mocho: <https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#> .\n@prefix owl:   <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#> .\n@prefix rdfs:  <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .\n\n###  mocho:ImmovableWork\nmocho:ImmovableWork rdf:type owl:Class ;\n    rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work ;\n    rdfs:label \"Immovable Work\"@en ;\n    rdfs:comment \"\"\"Permanent, fixed, non-transportable tangible assets of significant\n        historical, architectural, or archaeological value, including buildings, sites,\n        monuments, and urban or rural ensembles. Distinct from moveable built objects\n        (furniture, stage sets) which remain vra:Work. Analogous to CIDOC-CRM\n        crm:E25 Human-Made Feature.\"\"\"@en .\n\n###  mocho:ImageWork\nmocho:ImageWork rdf:type owl:Class ;\n    rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work ;\n    rdfs:label \"Image Work\"@en ;\n    rdfs:comment \"\"\"A photographic or visual image artifact treated as a Work-level entity:\n        the photograph, slide, negative, or postcard is itself the creative artifact\n        collected and described. Distinct from vra:Image (used for documentation images\n        of artworks; also Work level per ┬ž1.1.1) in that mocho:ImageWork makes the\n        Work-level WEMI commitment explicit via its subClassOf chain.\"\"\"@en .\n\n###  mocho:ImageObject\nmocho:ImageObject rdf:type owl:Class ;\n    rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Manifestation ;\n    rdfs:label \"Image Object\"@en ;\n    rdfs:comment \"\"\"A digital image carrier at Manifestation level: a scan, online image\n        file, or digital reproduction. Used for WebResources (edm:isShownBy /\n        edm:hasView targets) of mt002 (Photo) records. Subsumes the visual-surrogate\n        role of vra:Image at the carrier level.\"\"\"@en .\n\n###  mocho:facsimile\n###  OUT OF SCOPE for current phase ÔÇö add in follow-up ADR\n###  Potential use case: sparte002 (Library) ÔÇö digital reproductions of manuscripts,\n###  early printed books, etc. are facsimiles of the physical originals.\n###\n###  mocho:facsimile rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty ;\n###      rdfs:label \"facsimile of\"@en ;\n###      rdfs:comment \"\"\"The subject is a more or less precise re-rendering, reproduction\n###          or visual representation of the object. A photograph of a drawing taken for\n###          exhibition in a catalog would be the typical example. It does not imply\n###          exactness of reproduction: a lower-resolution thumbnail can be a facsimile,\n###          for example. (After Hayes & Warren 2010 mw:facsimileOf, p. 74.)\"\"\"@en ;\n###      rdfs:domain mocho:ImageObject ;\n###      rdfs:range  owl:Thing .\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. Object-type categorization (dc:type ├Ś sector Ôćĺ rdf:type)\n\n### Reference tables\n\n**Sector (sparte)**\n\n| Code | Sector |\n|---|---|\n| sparte001 | Archive |\n| sparte002 | Library |\n| sparte003 | Monument Preservation |\n| sparte004 | Research |\n| sparte005 | Media Library |\n| sparte006 | Museum |\n| sparte007 | Others |\n\n**Mediatype (mt)**\n\n| Code | Mediatype |\n|---|---|\n| mt001 | Audio |\n| mt002 | Photo |\n| mt003 | Text |\n| mt005 | Video |\n| mt007 | Not Digitized |\n\nThis plan covers **mt002 (Photo)** only.\n\nFrom corpus analysis (`output/dctype_frequency_all.csv` and `image_type2class.json`).\n\n### Group A ÔÇö 2D Visual Artworks Ôćĺ `vra:Work`\n\nProvidedCHO is the creative/physical artwork.\n\n```python\nARTWORK_2D = {\n    \"Zeichnung\", \"Album Zeichnung\", \"Handzeichnung\", \"Zeichnung/Druckgrafik\",\n    \"Druckgraphik\", \"Druckgrafik\", \"Druckschrift\",\n    \"Grafik\", \"Graphik\",\n    \"Studie\", \"Skizze\", \"Silhouettenbild\", \"Zyklus\",\n    \"Gem├Ąlde\", \"Malerei\", \"Wandbild\", \"Buchmalerei\",\n    \"Plakat\", \"Mappenwerk\", \"Kost├╝mentwurf\",\n    \"Flugblatt\", \"Einzelblattsammlung\", \"Akzidenzdruck (Verlagsanzeige)\",\n    \"Rollenportr├Ąt\", \"Portr├Ąt\",      # in sparte005 (Media Library) / sparte006 (Museum)\n}\n```\n\n`Druck` in sparte006 (Museum) Ôćĺ `vra:Work` (printed artwork). In sparte005 (Media Library) Ôćĺ `mocho:ImageWork` (photographic print).\n\n### Group B ÔÇö 3D Objects Ôćĺ `vra:Work`\n\n```python\nOBJECTS_3D = {\n    \"Skulptur\", \"B├╝ste\", \"Relief\",\n    \"Medaille\", \"M├╝nze\", \"Geldschein\", \"Notgeldschein\",\n    \"Denkmal\",\n}\n```\n\n### Group C ÔÇö Photographic Works Ôćĺ `mocho:ImageWork`\n\nProvidedCHO is the photographic artifact: the photograph is the Work in DDB.\nApplies to sectors sparte005 (Media Library) and sparte006 (Museum).\n\n```python\n# All sectors in {sparte005, sparte006}\nPHOTO_TYPES = {\n    \"Fotografie\", \"Foto\",\n    \"Standfoto\", \"Kontaktbogen\", \"Kleinbilddia\",\n    \"Negativ\", \"Negativ s/w\", \"Positiv\",\n    \"Reprofotografie\", \"Fotoalbum\",\n    \"Fotomechanische Reproduktion\",\n    \"Bilddokument\",\n    \"Ansichtskarte\", \"Ansichtskarte / Motivkarte\",\n    \"Ansichtskarte / Motivkarte;Weltpostkarte\",\n    \"Postkarte\",\n}\n\n# sparte005 (Media Library) only ÔÇö sector-gated\nPHOTO_TYPES_MEDIA_LIBRARY_ONLY = {\n    \"Bild\",   # too generic in other sectors\n    \"Druck\",  # photographic print in Media Library; printed artwork in Museum (Ôćĺ vra:Work via ARTWORK_2D)\n}\n```\n\nDispatch logic: for `Bild` and `Druck`, check sector first: sparte005 Ôćĺ `mocho:ImageWork`; sparte006 Ôćĺ falls through to ARTWORK_2D or sector default (`vra:Work`).\n\n### Group D ÔÇö Architectural / Built Heritage Ôćĺ `mocho:ImmovableWork`\n\nApplies to **any sector** ÔÇö Denkmal, Baudenkmal appear across sparte003/004/005/007.\n\n`mocho:ImmovableWork rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work`: built heritage fixed in the environment\n(buildings, monuments, memorials). Distinct from moveable built objects (furniture, stage\nsets) which remain `vra:Work`. Analogous to CIDOC-CRM `crm:E25 Human-Made Feature`.\n\n```python\nARCHITECTURE = {\n    \"Baudenkmal\", \"Wohnhaus\", \"Museum\", \"Schule\", \"Universit├Ątsinstitut\",\n    \"Denkmal\",\n}\n```\n\n### Group E ÔÇö Archival Documents Ôćĺ `rico:Record` (via htype dispatch ÔÇö already handled)\n\nArchive photos (sparte001 + dc:type Ôłł PHOTO_TYPES) Ôćĺ **`rico:Record` only**. No image class\nis added: archive photographs may or may not be creative works (documentary, bureaucratic,\nadministrative images are common); asserting `mocho:ImageObject` (Manifestation) or\n`mocho:ImageWork` (Work) would over-specify WEMI level. The htype dispatch already gives\n`rico:Record`; no additional dc:type layer fires for sparte001 + PHOTO_TYPES.\n\n### Group F ÔÇö Default Ôćĺ `mocho:Manifestation` (D9)\n\nAll remaining dc:types: Buch, Programmheft, Karte, Illustration (bibliographic), Seite, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 3. rdf:type dispatch model\n\n### 3.1 Revised D9: ProvidedCHO typing rule\n\n| Case | ProvidedCHO rdf:type | Condition |\n|---|---|---|\n| 3.1.1 Artwork 2D | `vra:Work` | dc:type Ôłł ARTWORK_2D; sector Ôłł {sparte005, sparte006} (Media Library, Museum) |\n| 3.1.2 3D Object | `vra:Work` | dc:type Ôłł OBJECTS_3D; any sector |\n| 3.1.3 Photo Work | `mocho:ImageWork` | dc:type Ôłł PHOTO_TYPES; sector Ôłł {sparte005, sparte006} (Media Library, Museum) |\n| 3.1.4 Archive photo | `rico:Record` (htype dispatch only) | sparte001 + dc:type Ôłł PHOTO_TYPES ÔÇö no image WEMI class added |\n| 3.1.5 Architecture | `mocho:ImmovableWork` | dc:type Ôłł ARCHITECTURE; any sector |\n| 3.1.6 Default | `mocho:Manifestation` (D9) | all remaining |\n\nWebResource Ôćĺ **always** `mocho:ImageObject` for mt002 records.\n\n### 3.2 Turtle patterns: ProvidedCHO\n\n**3.2.1 Museum/Media Library artwork (Zeichnung, Gem├Ąlde, Druckgraphik)**\n```turtle\n@prefix vra: <http://purl.org/vra/> .\n\n<cho-uri> a vra:Work .\n```\n\n**3.2.2 Museum/Media Library photo (Fotografie, Standfoto, Postkarte)**\n```turtle\n@prefix mocho: <https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#> .\n\n<cho-uri> a mocho:ImageWork .\n# mocho:ImageWork rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work Ôćĺ Work membership by inference\n```\n\n**3.2.3 Archive photo (sparte001 + PHOTO_TYPES)**\n\nNo additional class beyond the htype dispatch result. WEMI level is not asserted.\n\n```turtle\n@prefix rico: <https://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#> .\n\n<cho-uri> a rico:Record .   # from htype dispatch; no image WEMI class added\n```\n\n### 3.3 Turtle pattern: WebResource (digital carrier)\n\nApplied to the target URI of `edm:isShownBy` or `edm:hasView`.\n\n```turtle\n@prefix mocho: <https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#> .\n@prefix rdac:  <http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/> .\n@prefix rdam:  <http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/> .\n@prefix rdact: <http://rdaregistry.info/termList/RDAct/> .\n@prefix vra:   <http://purl.org/vra/> .\n\n<webresource-uri>\n    a mocho:ImageObject ;    # digital image carrier (subClassOf mocho:Manifestation)\n    a rdac:C10007 ;          # RDA Manifestation class\n    rdam:P30001 rdact:1018 ; # has carrier type: online resource\n    vra:imageOf <cho-uri> .  # interim link; replace with mocho:facsimile in Phase B\n```\n\n- `rdact:1018` = `http://rdaregistry.info/termList/RDAct/1018` ÔÇö \"online resource\"\n- `vra:imageOf` (`owl:inverseOf vra:hasImage`): links the digital image to the Work it documents. Used as interim; `mocho:facsimile` (domain: `mocho:ImageObject`, range: `mocho:ImageWork` / `vra:Work`) replaces it in Phase B (follow-up ADR).\n- `rdam:P30139` (\"has expression manifested\") was considered but is **Manifestation Ôćĺ Expression**, not Work. Dropped ÔÇö DDB does not model Expression-level entities.\n\n### 3.4 Sector-based defaults (dc:types not matched by any group)\n\n| Sector | Default ProvidedCHO type | Rationale |\n|---|---|---|\n| sparte006 (Museum) | `vra:Work` | Museum objects assumed to be artworks/3D objects |\n| sparte005 (Media Library) | `mocho:ImageWork` | Media Library objects assumed to be photo/image Works |\n| sparte003 (Monument Preservation) | `vra:Work` | Built heritage Ôćĺ provisional `vra:Work` |\n| sparte001 (Archive) | `mocho:Manifestation` (D9; htype handles `rico:Record`) | Archival records |\n| sparte002 (Library), sparte004 (Research), sparte007 (Others) | `mocho:Manifestation` (D9) | Bibliographic / mixed |\n\n---\n\n## 4. Open questions\n\n| # | Question | Status |\n|---|---|---|\n| 4.1 | `rdam:P30139` | **Resolved: wrong property** ÔÇö \"has expression manifested\" (Manifestation Ôćĺ Expression). Dropped; `vra:imageOf` used instead until `mocho:facsimile` is defined. |\n| 4.2 | Group D (architecture) | **Resolved: `mocho:ImmovableWork`** ÔÇö `rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work`; distinguishes fixed built heritage from moveable works. |\n| 4.3 | `mocho:facsimile` ÔÇö emit in transform for WebResource Ôćĺ CHO link? | **Resolved**: emit `vra:imageOf` now as interim; replace with `mocho:facsimile` in Phase B ADR |\n| 4.4 | WebResource typing: in scope for current plan or separate phase? | **Resolved**: in scope ÔÇö same script as ProvidedCHO dispatch |\n| 4.5 | Archive photo | **Resolved**: `rico:Record` only (htype dispatch); no image WEMI class. No additional row in lookup CSV for sparte001 + PHOTO_TYPES. |\n\n---\n\n## 5. Corpus impact\n\n| dc:type | total count | ProvidedCHO type |\n|---|---|---|\n| Zeichnung | 3379 | `vra:Work` (sparte005 Media Library, sparte006 Museum); `rico:Record` (sparte001 Archive) |\n| Fotografie | 3340 | `mocho:ImageWork` (sparte005/sparte006); `rico:Record` (sparte001, htype only) |\n| Druckgraphik | 2646 | `vra:Work` (sparte006 Museum, sparte002 Library) |\n| Druck | 986 | `vra:Work` (sparte006 Museum); `mocho:ImageWork` (sparte005 Media Library) |\n| Archivale | 924 | `rico:Record` (D9 via htype) |\n| Brief | 916 | `rico:Record` (D9 via htype) |\n| Gem├Ąlde | 599 | `vra:Work` (sparte006 Museum) |\n\nWebResources for all IMAGE/mt002 records Ôćĺ `mocho:ImageObject` + `rdac:C10007` + `rdam:P30001 rdact:1018` + `vra:imageOf <cho-uri>` (interim; Phase B: replace with `mocho:facsimile`)\n\n---\n\n## 6. Files to modify\n\n| File | Action |\n|---|---|\n| `mocho/mocho-odk/mocho/mocho-edit.owl` | **Manual edit** ÔÇö add `mocho:ImmovableWork`, `mocho:ImageWork`, `mocho:ImageObject` per OWL definitions in ┬ž1.4; update Work+Manifestation unions |\n| `scripts/gen_image_type2class.py` | Add ARTWORK_2D / OBJECTS_3D / PHOTO_TYPES / ARCHITECTURE; emit ProvidedCHO triples (`vra:Work` / `mocho:ImageWork` / etc.) **and** WebResource triples (`mocho:ImageObject` + `rdac:C10007` + `rdam:P30001 rdact:1018` + `vra:imageOf`) |\n| `output/config/image_type2class.json` | Regenerated output |\n| `notes/transform-script-plan.md` ┬ž3.3 | Update taxonomy |\n| `notes/alignment-adr.md` | New D11: revised D9 + `mocho:ImageWork`; D12: WebResource as `mocho:ImageObject` |\n| `notes/image-type-class-mapping.md` | Write this plan as a standalone mapping reference note |\n| `mocho/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Add sparte/sector + mediatype reference table |\n| `babel-ddb/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Add sparte/sector + mediatype reference table |\n| `gemea/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Add sparte/sector + mediatype reference table |\n| `goethe-faust/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Add sparte/sector + mediatype reference table |\n\n---\n\n## 7. ## Approved Plan:
# Plan: Map DDB IMAGE Objects to mocho ÔÇö VRA Core + mocho:ImageWork / mocho:ImageObject

## 0. Context

DDB records with `edm:type = "IMAGE"` and `mediatype = mt002` span ontologically
distinct categories. The existing approach (D9: every ProvidedCHO = `mocho:Manifestation`)
is incorrect for museum visual objects.

Revised model ÔÇö two new native mocho classes:

| Entity | Class | WEMI level |
|---|---|---|
| Artwork ProvidedCHO (Zeichnung, Gem├Ąlde) | `vra:Work` | Work |
| Photo ProvidedCHO (Fotografie, Standfoto) | `mocho:ImageWork` | Work |
| WebResource (digital scan / image file) | `mocho:ImageObject` | Manifestation |

`vra:Image` is aligned to Manifestation in the babel-ddb paper ("visual surrogate or
digital reproduction"); `vra:Image rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork` in the OWL
(WEMI-neutral), but the semantic intent is documentation image, not photo-as-Work.
Therefore `vra:Image` is NOT used for ProvidedCHOs. `mocho:ImageObject` subsumes its
Manifestation role at the WebResource level.

---

## 1. Conceptual framework

### 1.1 VRA Core 4.0 classes (from `mocho/ontology/vra-core-v4.0.owl`)

IRI base: `http://purl.org/vra/`

- **`vra:Work`** ÔÇö `rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork`. The artwork as a creative/physical entity.
  2D artworks (Zeichnung, Gem├Ąlde), 3D sculptures, architecture.
- **`vra:Image`** ÔÇö `rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork`. A visual image artifact (photograph,
  slide, documentation image). **Work level** ÔÇö see ┬ž1.1.1 for the full discussion.
- **`vra:imageOf`** ÔÇö ObjectProperty, `owl:inverseOf vra:hasImage`. Links a `vra:Image` to
  the `vra:Work` it documents.

#### 1.1.1 Discussion: WEMI level of `vra:Image`

**The babel-ddb alignment (now revised)**

The babel-ddb paper placed `vra:Image` at **Manifestation** level using a structural argument:
VRA Core's tripartite schema (Work Ôćĺ Image, no intermediate Expression) collapses the FRBR
WÔćĺEÔćĺM hierarchy, so Image occupies the slot where Manifestation would be. Under this reading,
`vra:Image` is a visual surrogate or digital reproduction of a `vra:Work` ÔÇö a carrier, not
a creative entity.

**The problem: `schema:CreativeWork` is not WEMI-aligned**

`vra:Image rdfs:subClassOf schema:CreativeWork`. Schema.org has no WEMI hierarchy: its
`schema:CreativeWork` covers books, paintings, photographs, recordings, and software ÔÇö entities
that span all FRBR levels. Schema.org classes are **WEMI-neutral by design**, but they carry
**WEMI implications through naming and semantic intent**: "CreativeWork" reads as a creative
or intellectual entity, which is Work-level intuition. A reasoner that maps
`schema:CreativeWork Ôćĺ mocho:Work` would infer `vra:Image ÔŐĹ mocho:Work`, directly
contradicting the babel-ddb Manifestation placement.

**Hayes & Warren** compound the ambiguity: *"In many cases the subject image itself will be
the work."* A photograph is a creative act; the photographer made deliberate choices. From
this angle, a `vra:Image` (a photograph) is unambiguously a Work.

**VRA Core's own design intent** supports the Work reading too: VRA describes visual arts
collections from the perspective of image creators. `vra:Work` is the collected artifact;
`vra:Image` is the visual record of it ÔÇö but the photograph of an artwork is itself an
artifact created by someone, not merely a carrier. VRA does not call `vra:Image` a
Manifestation anywhere in its specification.

**Conclusion and revised alignment**

`vra:Image` is treated as **Work level** in this plan, consistent with:
- Its OWL superclass `schema:CreativeWork`
- VRA Core's creator-centric framing
- Hayes & Warren's observation that images are often Works in their own right

The babel-ddb structural argument (Manifestation slot in a collapsed hierarchy) is noted
but not adopted here. mocho follows the model, not the babel-ddb paper. The key implication:
`vra:Image` belongs in the `mocho:Work` union (not Manifestation); WebResources are typed
with `mocho:ImageObject` (`rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Manifestation`) to make the carrier level
explicit without relying on `vra:Image`.

### 1.2 LIO (from `mocho/odk-ontology/lio_import.owl`)

IRI base: `https://w3id.org/lio/v1#`

- **`lio:depicts`**, **`lio:shows`** ÔÇö kept for property-level alignment; not used for rdf:type dispatch.
- **`lio:Image`** ÔÇö NOT used for rdf:type dispatch (see ┬ž1.2.1).
- **`mw:facsimile`** ÔÇö Hayes & Warren 2010 (`http://purl.org/net/mw/`).
  `facsimileOf (Image, Image)`: the subject is a reproduction of the object.
  `lio:fascimile` appeared in rm024-lio.pdf slides but was never implemented in the LIO OWL.
  **Resolution**: define `mocho:facsimile` natively (see ┬ž1.3.3). Follow-up ADR.

#### 1.2.1 Decision: `lio:Image` vs `mocho:ImageWork` for photo ProvidedCHOs

**Pros of `lio:Image`**
- Already imported in mocho (`lio_import.owl`) ÔÇö no new class definition required
- Designed specifically for image description; semantically appropriate for photographs
- WEMI-neutral in the LIO OWL ÔÇö does not over-commit to Work or Manifestation
- Already in the mocho:Work union (prior state of mocho-edit.owl)
- Broad definition: "any PictorialElement considered to be a complete image" ÔÇö covers photos, drawings, paintings

**Cons of `lio:Image`**
- Research ontology; not widely adopted in production CH linked data pipelines
- WEMI-neutrality is also a weakness: the class gives no signal about level; dispatch logic must always pair it with `mocho:Work` or `mocho:Manifestation` at instance level
- Semantically overlaps with `vra:Image` and `foaf:Image`; unclear precedence
- `lio:fascimile` was planned in LIO but never implemented ÔÇö the OWL is incomplete, raising confidence concerns about the namespace long-term
- Mixes an image-description ontology (LIO) with CH-domain ontologies (VRA, RDA, RiC-O) in the same dispatch layer

**Note on `vra:Image` as Manifestation**

The babel-ddb paper aligns `vra:Image` to Manifestation level (structural argument: VRA collapses
WÔćĺEÔćĺM to WÔćĺImage, so Image occupies the Manifestation slot). However, `vra:Image rdfs:subClassOf
schema:CreativeWork` in the OWL ÔÇö and `schema:CreativeWork` reads as a Work-level entity by name
and Schema.org intent (though Schema.org has no WEMI at all). A reasoner that maps
`schema:CreativeWork Ôćĺ mocho:Work` would infer `vra:Image ÔŐĹ mocho:Work`, directly contradicting
the babel-ddb Manifestation placement. `vra:Image` is therefore ontologically unreliable for
explicit WEMI dispatch in mocho: its OWL superclass pulls toward Work; its VRA design intent
and babel-ddb alignment pull toward Manifestation.

**Final decision: `mocho:ImageWork`**

Reasons:
- `rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work` gives explicit, unambiguous WEMI commitment ÔÇö no contradiction from inherited superclasses
- Pairs symmetrically with `mocho:ImageObject` (`rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Manifestation`) for WebResources ÔÇö both have mocho-native, clean WEMI chains
- Avoids the `vra:Image` / `schema:CreativeWork` WEMI ambiguity entirely
- Native mocho class ÔÇö no external LIO dependency required for dispatch; LIO import retained only for property use (`lio:depicts`, `lio:shows`)
- Name is self-documenting in the mocho namespace; aligns with mocho's role as a hub ontology

### 1.3 New mocho classes

#### 1.3.1 `mocho:ImageWork` ÔÇö photographic Work

Photo ProvidedCHOs: the photograph is the creative artifact collected and described in DDB.

```turtle
mocho:ImageWork rdf:type owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work ;
    rdfs:label "Image Work"@en ;
    rdfs:comment """A photographic or visual image artifact treated as a Work-level entity:
        the photograph, slide, negative, or postcard is itself the creative artifact
        collected and described. Distinct from vra:Image (documentation image of an
        artwork, Manifestation level)."""@en .
```

#### 1.3.2 `mocho:ImageObject` ÔÇö digital image carrier (Manifestation)

WebResources: the digital file that carries a Work (artwork or photo).

```turtle
mocho:ImageObject rdf:type owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Manifestation ;
    rdfs:label "Image Object"@en ;
    rdfs:comment """A digital image carrier at Manifestation level: a scan, online image
        file, or digital reproduction. Used exclusively for WebResources (edm:isShownBy /
        edm:hasView targets). Subsumes the vra:Image role (visual surrogate /
        documentation image)."""@en .
```

#### 1.3.3 `mocho:facsimile` ÔÇö reproduction link (follow-up ADR)

Source definition ÔÇö Hayes & Warren 2010, p. 74, Properties (Domain, Range):

> ***facsimileOf*** (Image, Image) ÔÇö "The subject is a more or less precise re-rendering,
> reproduction or visual representation of the object. A photograph of a drawing taken for
> exhibition in a catalog would be the typical example. It does not imply exactness of
> reproduction: a lower-resolution thumbnail can be a facsimile, for example."

```turtle
mocho:facsimile rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty ;
    rdfs:label "facsimile of"@en ;
    rdfs:comment """The subject is a more or less precise re-rendering, reproduction or
        visual representation of the object. A photograph of a drawing taken for exhibition
        in a catalog would be the typical example. It does not imply exactness of
        reproduction: a lower-resolution thumbnail can be a facsimile, for example.
        (After Hayes & Warren 2010 mw:facsimileOf, p. 74.)"""@en ;
    rdfs:domain mocho:ImageObject ;
    rdfs:range  owl:Thing .   # mocho:ImageWork or vra:Work
```

### 1.4 mocho-edit.owl changes required

**Summary of changes**

| Change | Detail |
|---|---|
| Define `mocho:ImmovableWork` | New OWL class, `rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work` |
| Define `mocho:ImageWork` | New OWL class, `rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work` |
| Define `mocho:ImageObject` | New OWL class, `rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Manifestation` |
| `mocho:Work` union | Add `mocho:ImageWork`; add `vra:Image` (Work level, revised); remove `lio:Image` |
| `mocho:Manifestation` union | Add `mocho:ImageObject`; remove `vra:Image` (no longer Manifestation) |
| Define `mocho:facsimile` | **Out of scope** ÔÇö not used in goethe-faust corpus; follow-up ADR only |

**OWL definitions to add to `mocho-edit.owl`**

```turtle
@prefix mocho: <https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#> .
@prefix owl:   <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#> .
@prefix rdfs:  <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .

###  mocho:ImmovableWork
mocho:ImmovableWork rdf:type owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work ;
    rdfs:label "Immovable Work"@en ;
    rdfs:comment """Permanent, fixed, non-transportable tangible assets of significant
        historical, architectural, or archaeological value, including buildings, sites,
        monuments, and urban or rural ensembles. Distinct from moveable built objects
        (furniture, stage sets) which remain vra:Work. Analogous to CIDOC-CRM
        crm:E25 Human-Made Feature."""@en .

###  mocho:ImageWork
mocho:ImageWork rdf:type owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work ;
    rdfs:label "Image Work"@en ;
    rdfs:comment """A photographic or visual image artifact treated as a Work-level entity:
        the photograph, slide, negative, or postcard is itself the creative artifact
        collected and described. Distinct from vra:Image (used for documentation images
        of artworks; also Work level per ┬ž1.1.1) in that mocho:ImageWork makes the
        Work-level WEMI commitment explicit via its subClassOf chain."""@en .

###  mocho:ImageObject
mocho:ImageObject rdf:type owl:Class ;
    rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Manifestation ;
    rdfs:label "Image Object"@en ;
    rdfs:comment """A digital image carrier at Manifestation level: a scan, online image
        file, or digital reproduction. Used for WebResources (edm:isShownBy /
        edm:hasView targets) of mt002 (Photo) records. Subsumes the visual-surrogate
        role of vra:Image at the carrier level."""@en .

###  mocho:facsimile
###  OUT OF SCOPE for current phase ÔÇö add in follow-up ADR
###  Potential use case: sparte002 (Library) ÔÇö digital reproductions of manuscripts,
###  early printed books, etc. are facsimiles of the physical originals.
###
###  mocho:facsimile rdf:type owl:ObjectProperty ;
###      rdfs:label "facsimile of"@en ;
###      rdfs:comment """The subject is a more or less precise re-rendering, reproduction
###          or visual representation of the object. A photograph of a drawing taken for
###          exhibition in a catalog would be the typical example. It does not imply
###          exactness of reproduction: a lower-resolution thumbnail can be a facsimile,
###          for example. (After Hayes & Warren 2010 mw:facsimileOf, p. 74.)"""@en ;
###      rdfs:domain mocho:ImageObject ;
###      rdfs:range  owl:Thing .
```

---

## 2. Object-type categorization (dc:type ├Ś sector Ôćĺ rdf:type)

### Reference tables

**Sector (sparte)**

| Code | Sector |
|---|---|
| sparte001 | Archive |
| sparte002 | Library |
| sparte003 | Monument Preservation |
| sparte004 | Research |
| sparte005 | Media Library |
| sparte006 | Museum |
| sparte007 | Others |

**Mediatype (mt)**

| Code | Mediatype |
|---|---|
| mt001 | Audio |
| mt002 | Photo |
| mt003 | Text |
| mt005 | Video |
| mt007 | Not Digitized |

This plan covers **mt002 (Photo)** only.

From corpus analysis (`output/dctype_frequency_all.csv` and `image_type2class.json`).

### Group A ÔÇö 2D Visual Artworks Ôćĺ `vra:Work`

ProvidedCHO is the creative/physical artwork.

```python
ARTWORK_2D = {
    "Zeichnung", "Album Zeichnung", "Handzeichnung", "Zeichnung/Druckgrafik",
    "Druckgraphik", "Druckgrafik", "Druckschrift",
    "Grafik", "Graphik",
    "Studie", "Skizze", "Silhouettenbild", "Zyklus",
    "Gem├Ąlde", "Malerei", "Wandbild", "Buchmalerei",
    "Plakat", "Mappenwerk", "Kost├╝mentwurf",
    "Flugblatt", "Einzelblattsammlung", "Akzidenzdruck (Verlagsanzeige)",
    "Rollenportr├Ąt", "Portr├Ąt",      # in sparte005 (Media Library) / sparte006 (Museum)
}
```

`Druck` in sparte006 (Museum) Ôćĺ `vra:Work` (printed artwork). In sparte005 (Media Library) Ôćĺ `mocho:ImageWork` (photographic print).

### Group B ÔÇö 3D Objects Ôćĺ `vra:Work`

```python
OBJECTS_3D = {
    "Skulptur", "B├╝ste", "Relief",
    "Medaille", "M├╝nze", "Geldschein", "Notgeldschein",
    "Denkmal",
}
```

### Group C ÔÇö Photographic Works Ôćĺ `mocho:ImageWork`

ProvidedCHO is the photographic artifact: the photograph is the Work in DDB.
Applies to sectors sparte005 (Media Library) and sparte006 (Museum).

```python
# All sectors in {sparte005, sparte006}
PHOTO_TYPES = {
    "Fotografie", "Foto",
    "Standfoto", "Kontaktbogen", "Kleinbilddia",
    "Negativ", "Negativ s/w", "Positiv",
    "Reprofotografie", "Fotoalbum",
    "Fotomechanische Reproduktion",
    "Bilddokument",
    "Ansichtskarte", "Ansichtskarte / Motivkarte",
    "Ansichtskarte / Motivkarte;Weltpostkarte",
    "Postkarte",
}

# sparte005 (Media Library) only ÔÇö sector-gated
PHOTO_TYPES_MEDIA_LIBRARY_ONLY = {
    "Bild",   # too generic in other sectors
    "Druck",  # photographic print in Media Library; printed artwork in Museum (Ôćĺ vra:Work via ARTWORK_2D)
}
```

Dispatch logic: for `Bild` and `Druck`, check sector first: sparte005 Ôćĺ `mocho:ImageWork`; sparte006 Ôćĺ falls through to ARTWORK_2D or sector default (`vra:Work`).

### Group D ÔÇö Architectural / Built Heritage Ôćĺ `mocho:ImmovableWork`

Applies to **any sector** ÔÇö Denkmal, Baudenkmal appear across sparte003/004/005/007.

`mocho:ImmovableWork rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work`: built heritage fixed in the environment
(buildings, monuments, memorials). Distinct from moveable built objects (furniture, stage
sets) which remain `vra:Work`. Analogous to CIDOC-CRM `crm:E25 Human-Made Feature`.

```python
ARCHITECTURE = {
    "Baudenkmal", "Wohnhaus", "Museum", "Schule", "Universit├Ątsinstitut",
    "Denkmal",
}
```

### Group E ÔÇö Archival Documents Ôćĺ `rico:Record` (via htype dispatch ÔÇö already handled)

Archive photos (sparte001 + dc:type Ôłł PHOTO_TYPES) Ôćĺ **`rico:Record` only**. No image class
is added: archive photographs may or may not be creative works (documentary, bureaucratic,
administrative images are common); asserting `mocho:ImageObject` (Manifestation) or
`mocho:ImageWork` (Work) would over-specify WEMI level. The htype dispatch already gives
`rico:Record`; no additional dc:type layer fires for sparte001 + PHOTO_TYPES.

### Group F ÔÇö Default Ôćĺ `mocho:Manifestation` (D9)

All remaining dc:types: Buch, Programmheft, Karte, Illustration (bibliographic), Seite, etc.

---

## 3. rdf:type dispatch model

### 3.1 Revised D9: ProvidedCHO typing rule

| Case | ProvidedCHO rdf:type | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| 3.1.1 Artwork 2D | `vra:Work` | dc:type Ôłł ARTWORK_2D; sector Ôłł {sparte005, sparte006} (Media Library, Museum) |
| 3.1.2 3D Object | `vra:Work` | dc:type Ôłł OBJECTS_3D; any sector |
| 3.1.3 Photo Work | `mocho:ImageWork` | dc:type Ôłł PHOTO_TYPES; sector Ôłł {sparte005, sparte006} (Media Library, Museum) |
| 3.1.4 Archive photo | `rico:Record` (htype dispatch only) | sparte001 + dc:type Ôłł PHOTO_TYPES ÔÇö no image WEMI class added |
| 3.1.5 Architecture | `mocho:ImmovableWork` | dc:type Ôłł ARCHITECTURE; any sector |
| 3.1.6 Default | `mocho:Manifestation` (D9) | all remaining |

WebResource Ôćĺ **always** `mocho:ImageObject` for mt002 records.

### 3.2 Turtle patterns: ProvidedCHO

**3.2.1 Museum/Media Library artwork (Zeichnung, Gem├Ąlde, Druckgraphik)**
```turtle
@prefix vra: <http://purl.org/vra/> .

<cho-uri> a vra:Work .
```

**3.2.2 Museum/Media Library photo (Fotografie, Standfoto, Postkarte)**
```turtle
@prefix mocho: <https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#> .

<cho-uri> a mocho:ImageWork .
# mocho:ImageWork rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work Ôćĺ Work membership by inference
```

**3.2.3 Archive photo (sparte001 + PHOTO_TYPES)**

No additional class beyond the htype dispatch result. WEMI level is not asserted.

```turtle
@prefix rico: <https://www.ica.org/standards/RiC/ontology#> .

<cho-uri> a rico:Record .   # from htype dispatch; no image WEMI class added
```

### 3.3 Turtle pattern: WebResource (digital carrier)

Applied to the target URI of `edm:isShownBy` or `edm:hasView`.

```turtle
@prefix mocho: <https://ise-fizkarlsruhe.github.io/ddbkg/mocho#> .
@prefix rdac:  <http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/> .
@prefix rdam:  <http://rdaregistry.info/Elements/m/> .
@prefix rdact: <http://rdaregistry.info/termList/RDAct/> .
@prefix vra:   <http://purl.org/vra/> .

<webresource-uri>
    a mocho:ImageObject ;    # digital image carrier (subClassOf mocho:Manifestation)
    a rdac:C10007 ;          # RDA Manifestation class
    rdam:P30001 rdact:1018 ; # has carrier type: online resource
    vra:imageOf <cho-uri> .  # interim link; replace with mocho:facsimile in Phase B
```

- `rdact:1018` = `http://rdaregistry.info/termList/RDAct/1018` ÔÇö "online resource"
- `vra:imageOf` (`owl:inverseOf vra:hasImage`): links the digital image to the Work it documents. Used as interim; `mocho:facsimile` (domain: `mocho:ImageObject`, range: `mocho:ImageWork` / `vra:Work`) replaces it in Phase B (follow-up ADR).
- `rdam:P30139` ("has expression manifested") was considered but is **Manifestation Ôćĺ Expression**, not Work. Dropped ÔÇö DDB does not model Expression-level entities.

### 3.4 Sector-based defaults (dc:types not matched by any group)

| Sector | Default ProvidedCHO type | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| sparte006 (Museum) | `vra:Work` | Museum objects assumed to be artworks/3D objects |
| sparte005 (Media Library) | `mocho:ImageWork` | Media Library objects assumed to be photo/image Works |
| sparte003 (Monument Preservation) | `vra:Work` | Built heritage Ôćĺ provisional `vra:Work` |
| sparte001 (Archive) | `mocho:Manifestation` (D9; htype handles `rico:Record`) | Archival records |
| sparte002 (Library), sparte004 (Research), sparte007 (Others) | `mocho:Manifestation` (D9) | Bibliographic / mixed |

---

## 4. Open questions

| # | Question | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 4.1 | `rdam:P30139` | **Resolved: wrong property** ÔÇö "has expression manifested" (Manifestation Ôćĺ Expression). Dropped; `vra:imageOf` used instead until `mocho:facsimile` is defined. |
| 4.2 | Group D (architecture) | **Resolved: `mocho:ImmovableWork`** ÔÇö `rdfs:subClassOf mocho:Work`; distinguishes fixed built heritage from moveable works. |
| 4.3 | `mocho:facsimile` ÔÇö emit in transform for WebResource Ôćĺ CHO link? | **Resolved**: emit `vra:imageOf` now as interim; replace with `mocho:facsimile` in Phase B ADR |
| 4.4 | WebResource typing: in scope for current plan or separate phase? | **Resolved**: in scope ÔÇö same script as ProvidedCHO dispatch |
| 4.5 | Archive photo | **Resolved**: `rico:Record` only (htype dispatch); no image WEMI class. No additional row in lookup CSV for sparte001 + PHOTO_TYPES. |

## 5. Corpus impact

| dc:type | total count | ProvidedCHO type |
|---|---|---|
| Zeichnung | 3379 | `vra:Work` (sparte005 Media Library, sparte006 Museum); `rico:Record` (sparte001 Archive) |
| Fotografie | 3340 | `mocho:ImageWork` (sparte005/sparte006); `rico:Record` (sparte001, htype only) |
| Druckgraphik | 2646 | `vra:Work` (sparte006 Museum, sparte002 Library) |
| Druck | 986 | `vra:Work` (sparte006 Museum); `mocho:ImageWork` (sparte005 Media Library) |
| Archivale | 924 | `rico:Record` (D9 via htype) |
| Brief | 916 | `rico:Record` (D9 via htype) |
| Gem├Ąlde | 599 | `vra:Work` (sparte006 Museum) |

WebResources for all IMAGE/mt002 records Ôćĺ `mocho:ImageObject` + `rdac:C10007` + `rdam:P30001 rdact:1018` + `vra:imageOf <cho-uri>` (interim; Phase B: replace with `mocho:facsimile`)

### User *(2026-04-17T14:22:15.732Z)*

Base directory for this skill: /Users/mta/Documents/claude/babel-ddb/.claude/skills/senior_python_developer

You are now acting as a **senior Python developer** specializing in NLP pipelines, data engineering, and API development. Apply this lens to all Python work in this session.

## Role priorities

### Readability
- Type annotations on every function signature and class attribute; no bare `Any` without a comment
- Descriptive names: `extracted_title` not `t`, `provider_batch` not `pb`; single-letter names only as loop indices
- Public functions and classes have docstrings: one-line summary + Args/Returns for non-trivial signatures
- Functions ÔëĄ 40 lines; classes have a single responsibility; split at natural seams
- Every `scripts/py/` file opens with the standard header block: purpose, usage, inputs/outputs, dependencies, assumptions

### Reproducibility
- Virtual environment per project; deps pinned in `requirements.txt` (pip-compile) or `pyproject.toml`
- CLI scripts use `argparse` (or `typer`) ÔÇö no magic env vars as the primary interface
- Fix random seeds explicitly: `random.seed(SEED)`, `np.random.seed(SEED)`, `torch.manual_seed(SEED)`
- Structured logging with the `logging` module (not `print`); include timestamps, module name, level
- Output filenames encode key params (e.g., `silver_spans_tier2_n4613.jsonl`) ÔÇö no silent overwrites

### Scalability
- Generators and iterators for large corpora; never load 65M records into memory at once
- Async I/O (`asyncio` + `aiohttp`) for API-bound work; use `asyncio.Semaphore` for rate limiting
- Configurable `--batch-size`; default tuned for typical RAM and documented in argparse help
- SPARQL and ES queries paginate; never assume all results fit in one response
- Profile with `cProfile` or `memory_profiler` before optimizing hot paths

### Resource optimization
- Stream JSONL rather than loading JSON arrays; use `ijson` for large JSON files
- Avoid row-by-row pandas iteration; use vectorized operations or `apply` with a comment
- Connection pooling for ES (persistent `Elasticsearch` client) and SPARQL (reuse `requests.Session`)
- Use `__slots__` on dataclasses instantiated millions of times
- Release large objects explicitly (`del df; gc.collect()`) in long-running scripts

## Code quality standards
- Tests alongside code: `ingest/tests/` mirrors `ingest/`; `scripts/py/tests/` mirrors `scripts/py/`
- Happy path + edge cases + failure modes per function
- Unit tests mock external APIs; integration tests use real services via testcontainers
- `mypy --strict` clean; `ruff` for linting (replaces flake8 + isort + pyupgrade)
- No hardcoded secrets ÔÇö env vars via `python-dotenv`; validate at startup

## Red flags ÔÇö always push back on these
- `subprocess(shell=True)` with user input Ôćĺ injection risk; use list args
- `eval()` or `exec()` on external data Ôćĺ never
- Mutable default arguments (`def f(x=[])`) Ôćĺ use `None` sentinel
- Catching bare `Exception` without re-raising or logging
- `os.system()` Ôćĺ use `subprocess.run(check=True)`
- Global state mutation in library code

## GeMeA context
See `.claude/gemea_pipeline.md` for the current phase order, script locations (`scripts/py/`, `ingest/`, `api/`), directory layout, external service endpoints and rate limits, tech stack, and key notes.

Read `gemea_pipeline.md` to understand which pipeline phase you are working in, what the upstream inputs are, and what the downstream consumers expect before writing or editing any script or module.

## Confirm role
Acknowledge this role, read `.claude/gemea_pipeline.md`, and state which pipeline phase and script/module you are working on.

    36Ôćĺdef load_mt002_rows():
    37Ôćĺ    rows = []
    38Ôćĺ    with FREQ_CSV.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
    39Ôćĺ        for r in csv.DictReader(f):
    40Ôćĺ            if "mt002" in r["mediatype"]:
    41Ôćĺ                rows.append(r)
    42Ôćĺ    return rows
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ
    45Ôćĺdef print_summary(rows):
    46Ôćĺ    by_sec = defaultdict(int)
    47Ôćĺ    totals = defaultdict(int)
    48Ôćĺ    for r in rows:
    49Ôćĺ        sec = r["sector"].rsplit("/", 1)[-1]
    50Ôćĺ        by_sec[sec] += int(r["count"])
    51Ôćĺ        totals[r["dc_type_de"]] += int(r["count"])
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ    print("=== mt002 sector totals ===")
    54Ôćĺ    for s, n in sorted(by_sec.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
    55Ôćĺ        print(f"  {s:<14} {n:>7}")
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ    print(f"\n=== Top 30 dc:types (all sectors), {len(totals)} unique total ===")
    58Ôćĺ    for dc, n in sorted(totals.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:30]:
    59Ôćĺ        print(f"  {dc:<50} {n:>7}")
    60Ôćĺ
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺdef main():
    63Ôćĺ    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    64Ôćĺ    parser.add_argument("--summary", action="store_true")
    65Ôćĺ    args = parser.parse_args()
    66Ôćĺ
    67Ôćĺ    rows = load_mt002_rows()
    68Ôćĺ
    69Ôćĺ    if args.summary:
    70Ôćĺ        print_summary(rows)
    71Ôćĺ        return
    72Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ    # Group by (dc_type_de, sector)
    74Ôćĺ    groups: dict = {}
    75Ôćĺ    for r in rows:
    76Ôćĺ        dc = r["dc_type_de"].strip()
    77Ôćĺ        sec = r["sector"].rsplit("/", 1)[-1]
    78Ôćĺ        key = (dc, sec)
    79Ôćĺ        groups.setdefault(key, {"count": 0})
    80Ôćĺ        groups[key]["count"] += int(r["count"])
    81Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ    # Build result: one entry per (dc_type, sector) with sector-driven class
    83Ôćĺ    result = {}
    84Ôćĺ    for (dc, sec), info in sorted(groups.items(), key=lambda x: (-x[1]["count"], x[0][0])):
    85Ôćĺ        entry_key = f"{dc}__{sec}" if sec != "none" else dc
    86Ôćĺ        classes = SECTOR_CLASS.get(sec, DEFAULT_CLASS)
    87Ôćĺ        result[entry_key] = {
    88Ôćĺ            "dc_type_de": dc,
    89Ôćĺ            "sector": f"http://ddb.vocnet.org/sparte/{sec}" if sec != "none" else "any",
    90Ôćĺ            "count": info["count"],
    91Ôćĺ            "class": [
    92Ôćĺ                "",           # W
    93Ôćĺ                "",           # E
    94Ôćĺ                classes,      # M ÔÇö list; gen_dctype_class_mapping.py uses first as primary
    95Ôćĺ                "",           # I
    96Ôćĺ            ],
    97Ôćĺ        }
    98Ôćĺ
    99Ôćĺ    OUTPUT.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   100Ôćĺ    OUTPUT.write_text(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=4), encoding="utf-8")
   101Ôćĺ
   102Ôćĺ    print(f"Wrote {len(result)} entries Ôćĺ {OUTPUT}")
   103Ôćĺ    print()
   104Ôćĺ    # Class distribution summary
   105Ôćĺ    class_counts: dict = defaultdict(int)
   106Ôćĺ    for entry in result.values():
   107Ôćĺ        m = entry["class"][2]
   108Ôćĺ        label = "+".join(m) if isinstance(m, list) else m
   109Ôćĺ        class_counts[label] += 1
   110Ôćĺ    print("Class distribution (M slot):")
   111Ôćĺ    for cls, n in sorted(class_counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
   112Ôćĺ        print(f"  {cls:<40} {n:>5} entries")
   113Ôćĺ
   114Ôćĺ
   115Ôćĺif __name__ == "__main__":
   116Ôćĺ    main()
   117Ôćĺ



```

     1Ôćĺ# Scripts
     2Ôćĺ
     3ÔćĺAll scripts live in `scripts/`. They use project-relative paths via
     4Ôćĺ`pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent`, so they can be run from
     5Ôćĺany working directory.
     6Ôćĺ
     7Ôćĺ---
     8Ôćĺ
     9Ôćĺ## Ontology alignment (run in order)
    10Ôćĺ
    11Ôćĺ### `profile_edm_fields.py`
    12ÔćĺProfiles all field keys present under `edm.RDF.*` entity types in the JSONL
    13Ôćĺdata file. Needed as input for `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`.
    14Ôćĺ
    15Ôćĺ- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
    16Ôćĺ- **Output**: `output/edm_field_profile.json`, `output/edm_field_profile.csv`
    17Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python3 scripts/profile_edm_fields.py`
    18Ôćĺ- **Notes**: Reports per-entity-type field names with record counts and
    19Ôćĺ  coverage percentages. Counts > 100% indicate array-valued entities
    20Ôćĺ  (multiple agents/concepts/events per record).
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ### `align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`
    23ÔćĺData-driven ontology alignment from DDB-EDM to mocho. Maps each EDM/DC
    24Ôćĺproperty actually present in the data to its corresponding RDA properties
    25Ôćĺin mocho, via the DCÔćĺRDA sub-property mapping from the mocho workflow.
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ- **Input**: `output/edm_field_profile.json` (from above),
    28Ôćĺ  `~/Documents/claude/mocho/ontology/ddbedm_1.0.ttl`,
    29Ôćĺ  `~/Documents/claude/mocho/mocho-odk/src/ontology/mocho-full.owl`,
    30Ôćĺ  `~/Documents/claude/mocho/output/mapping_dct_to_rda.csv`
    31Ôćĺ- **Output**: `output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.csv`,
    32Ôćĺ  `output/alignment_ddbedm_mocho.json`
    33Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python3 scripts/align_ddbedm_to_mocho.py`
    34Ôćĺ- **Dependencies**: `rdflib`
    35Ôćĺ- **Notes**:
    36Ôćĺ  - One CSV row per (edm_field ├Ś rda_property) pair; high-fanout DC terms
    37Ôćĺ    (e.g. `dc:creator` Ôćĺ 232 RDA sub-properties) produce many rows per field
    38Ôćĺ  - 32 DC/DCT fields matched to RDA properties in mocho (1,271 alignment rows)
    39Ôćĺ  - 55 unmatched fields fall into expected categories: EDM-structural
    40Ôćĺ    (`edm:isShownAt`, `edm:begin/end`), DDB extensions (`ddb:hierarchyType`,
    41Ôćĺ    `ddb:aggregationEntity`), SKOS labels, geo coordinates, and two known
    42Ôćĺ    DCTÔćĺRDA gaps (`dc:identifier`, `dcterms:spatial`)
    43Ôćĺ  - `dcTermSubject` (DDB data variant of `dcterms:subject`) handled via
    44Ôćĺ    hard-coded override in `OVERRIDES` dict
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ---
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ## Data pipeline (run in order)
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ### `gen_manifestation_types.py`
    51ÔćĺEmits `rdf:type` triples for every DDB object URI in the corpus. Items from
    52Ôćĺsector 2 (sparte002, library) or with a manifestation-level htype are typed as
    53Ôćĺ`rda:Manifestation` (`rdaregistry.info/Elements/c/C10007`); all others as
    54Ôćĺ`mocho:Manifestation`. Sector-2 check covers both the item's own
    55Ôćĺ`provider-info.domains` and parent institution domains.
    56Ôćĺ
    57Ôćĺ- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
    58Ôćĺ- **Output**: `output/mocho-goethe-faust.nt`
    59Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/gen_manifestation_types.py`
    60Ôćĺ- **Notes**: Manifestation htypes: htype_007 (Volume), htype_013 (Manuscript),
    61Ôćĺ  htype_014 (Issue), htype_020 (Multivolume Work), htype_021 (Monograph),
    62Ôćĺ  htype_025 (Review). Stats printed on completion.
    63Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ### `fetch-search-all.py`
    65ÔćĺFetches all DDB search results for the query "goethe" via the DDB Solr API
    66Ôćĺand merges them into a single JSON file.
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ- **Input**: DDB Solr API (live network access required)
    69Ôćĺ- **Output**: `data/ddb-search-goethe-all.json`
    70Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/fetch-search-all.py`
    71Ôćĺ- **Notes**: `TOTAL` must match the actual result count; update if the
    72Ôćĺ  dataset changes. 0.3 s delay between requests to avoid rate-limiting.
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ### `build_dataframe.py`
    75ÔćĺBuilds a flat per-object DataFrame from the raw JSONL.
    76Ôćĺ
    77Ôćĺ- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
    78Ôćĺ- **Output**: `output/items-dataframe.parquet` (115,398 ├Ś 10, 8.6 MB),
    79Ôćĺ  `output/items-dataframe-sample.csv` (first 500 rows)
    80Ôćĺ- **Columns**: `object_id`, `sector`, `provider_name`, `timespan_begin`,
    81Ôćĺ  `timespan_end`, `dc_type` (list), `dc_subject` (list), `metadata_format`,
    82Ôćĺ  `view_fields` (list), `digitized` (bool)
    83Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/build_dataframe.py`
    84Ôćĺ- **Dependencies**: `pandas`, `pyarrow`
    85Ôćĺ- **Notes**:
    86Ôćĺ  - `YEAR_RE` handles both free-text years and compact YYYYMMDD dates
    87Ôćĺ    (e.g. `18300213`) via two regex alternatives
    88Ôćĺ  - `timespan_begin` priority: `edm.RDF.TimeSpan.begin` Ôćĺ
    89Ôćĺ    `edm.RDF.TimeSpan.end` Ôćĺ `edm.RDF.ProvidedCHO.issued` (fallback)
    90Ôćĺ  - After fixes: 12,958 records (~11.2%) still lack a `timespan_begin` year
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ---
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ## Analysis scripts
    95Ôćĺ
    96Ôćĺ### `analyse_bucket.py`
    97ÔćĺReports top N dc:type and dc:subject values for records within a given
    98Ôćĺ`timespan_begin` year range. Reads from the parquet DataFrame.
    99Ôćĺ
   100Ôćĺ- **Input**: `output/items-dataframe.parquet`
   101Ôćĺ- **Output**: printed summary (or JSON with `--json`)
   102Ôćĺ- **Usage**:
   103Ôćĺ  ```
   104Ôćĺ  python scripts/analyse_bucket.py --start 2000 --end 2024
   105Ôćĺ  python scripts/analyse_bucket.py --start 2000 --end 2024 --top 10 --json
   106Ôćĺ  ```
   107Ôćĺ- **Dependencies**: `pandas`, `pyarrow`
   108Ôćĺ- **Notes**: Useful for characterising anomalous buckets in `fig_years.png`
   109Ôćĺ  (e.g. the 2000ÔÇô2024 spike is dominated by Goethe-Universit├Ąt Frankfurt
   110Ôćĺ  institutional records ÔÇö theses and working papers ÔÇö rather than cultural
   111Ôćĺ  heritage items)
   112Ôćĺ
   113Ôćĺ### `analyse_items.py`
   114ÔćĺAggregates item-level statistics across 6 dimensions from the raw JSONL.
   115Ôćĺ
   116Ôćĺ- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
   117Ôćĺ- **Output**: `output/items-analysis.json`
   118Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/analyse_items.py`
   119Ôćĺ
   120Ôćĺ### `analyse_years.py`
   121ÔćĺExtracts creation years from `edm.RDF.TimeSpan`, selects an optimal bucket
   122Ôćĺsize, and produces a bar chart. X-range restricted to 1600ÔÇôpresent; linear
   123Ôćĺy-scale.
   124Ôćĺ
   125Ôćĺ- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
   126Ôćĺ- **Output**: `output/fig_years.png`, `output/years-analysis.json`
   127Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/analyse_years.py`
   128Ôćĺ- **Notes**:
   129Ôćĺ  - `YEAR_RE` handles YYYYMMDD dates; 85.8% of records have a usable year
   130Ôćĺ  - Auto-selects bucket size (5 / 10 / 25 / 50 / 100 yr) to keep ÔëĄ 45 bins;
   131Ôćĺ    25-year buckets selected (18 non-empty bins from 1600+)
   132Ôćĺ  - Pre-1600 records (n = 2,551) omitted from chart; included in JSON
   133Ôćĺ  - Goethe era (1749ÔÇô1832) highlighted in red
   134Ôćĺ  - Bar counts: inside bars (white) for tall bars; above for short bars
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ### `audit_timespan_coverage.py`
   137ÔćĺAudits temporal coverage: for records missing a `TimeSpan` year, checks
   138Ôćĺwhether `dc:date`, `dc:issued` (and dcterms equivalents) in `ProvidedCHO`
   139Ôćĺcould provide a fallback year. Also reports all `ProvidedCHO` keys present
   140Ôćĺin those records, to identify further date-bearing fields.
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ- **Input**: `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
   143Ôćĺ- **Output**: printed summary (no files written)
   144Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/audit_timespan_coverage.py`
   145Ôćĺ- **Notes**: Led to discovery that `YEAR_RE` was broken for YYYYMMDD dates
   146Ôćĺ  and that `issued` is a viable fallback; both fixes applied to
   147Ôćĺ  `build_dataframe.py` and `analyse_years.py`
   148Ôćĺ
   149Ôćĺ### `match_objecttypes.py`
   150ÔćĺMaps DDB objecttype strings to FaBiO / DoCO ontology classes using a
   151Ôćĺ4-tier pipeline: exact match Ôćĺ translated exact match Ôćĺ Levenshtein Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺsentence embeddings.
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ- **Input**: `data/ddb-search-goethe-all.json`, `data/schemas/fabio.owl`,
   155Ôćĺ  `data/schemas/doco.owl`
   156Ôćĺ- **Output**: `output/ddb-type2fabio.json`
   157Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/match_objecttypes.py`
   158Ôćĺ- **Dependencies**: `deep-translator`, `rapidfuzz`, `sentence-transformers`,
   159Ôćĺ  `rdflib`
   160Ôćĺ
   161Ôćĺ### `summarise_results.py`
   162ÔćĺPrints a human-readable summary of `ddb-type2fabio.json`.
   163Ôćĺ
   164Ôćĺ- **Input**: `output/ddb-type2fabio.json`
   165Ôćĺ- **Output**: printed summary (no files written)
   166Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/summarise_results.py`
   167Ôćĺ
   168Ôćĺ---
   169Ôćĺ
   170Ôćĺ## Visualisation scripts
   171Ôćĺ
   172Ôćĺ### `visualise_items.py`
   173ÔćĺGenerates figures from `items-analysis.json` (German labels, pre-translation).
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ- **Input**: `output/items-analysis.json`
   176Ôćĺ- **Output**: `output/fig1_metadata_format.png`, `output/fig2_sparte.png`,
   177Ôćĺ  `output/fig3_providers_top20.png`, `output/fig4_dc_type_top20.png`,
   178Ôćĺ  `output/fig5_dc_subject_top30.png`, `output/fig6_view_fields_top20.png`
   179Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/visualise_items.py`
   180Ôćĺ- **Notes**: Labels remain in German. Use `translate_and_plot.py` or
   181Ôćĺ  `plot_latex_figs.py` for English-translated versions.
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ### `translate_and_plot.py`
   184ÔćĺTranslates German labels with Helsinki-NLP/opus-mt-de-en and regenerates
   185Ôćĺall figures with English labels. Reads from the parquet DataFrame.
   186Ôćĺ
   187Ôćĺ- **Input**: `output/items-dataframe.parquet`
   188Ôćĺ- **Output**: `output/fig2_sector.png`, `output/fig4_dc_type_top20.png`,
   189Ôćĺ  `output/fig5_dc_subject_top30.png`, `output/fig6_view_fields_top20.png`,
   190Ôćĺ  `output/dataset-summary.png`
   191Ôćĺ- **Usage**:
   192Ôćĺ  ```
   193Ôćĺ  HF_HOME=data/hf-cache HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 python scripts/translate_and_plot.py
   194Ôćĺ  ```
   195Ôćĺ- **Dependencies**: `transformers`, `pandas`, `pyarrow`, `matplotlib`
   196Ôćĺ- **Notes**: Model cached in `data/hf-cache/`. Manual overrides in `OVERRIDES`
   197Ôćĺ  dict for domain-specific terms (Hochschulschrift, Druckgraphik, etc.)
   198Ôćĺ
   199Ôćĺ### `plot_latex_figs.py`
   200ÔćĺRegenerates 4 figures as square 7├Ś7 in PNGs (1050├Ś1050 px) for a LaTeX
   201Ôćĺ2├Ś2 subfigure layout. Translates German labels via opus-mt-de-en.
   202Ôćĺ
   203Ôćĺ- **Input**: `output/items-dataframe.parquet`
   204Ôćĺ- **Output**: `output/fig1_metadata_format.png`, `output/fig2_sector.png`,
   205Ôćĺ  `output/fig4_dc_type_top20.png`, `output/fig5_dc_subject_top20.png`
   206Ôćĺ- **Usage**:
   207Ôćĺ  ```
   208Ôćĺ  HF_HOME=data/hf-cache HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 python scripts/plot_latex_figs.py
   209Ôćĺ  ```
   210Ôćĺ- **Dependencies**: `transformers`, `pandas`, `pyarrow`, `matplotlib`
   211Ôćĺ- **Notes**: `fig5` is top-20 subjects (not top-30). `fig1` uses explicit
   212Ôćĺ  subplot margins (not `bbox_inches="tight"`) to preserve square aspect ratio.
   213Ôćĺ
   214Ôćĺ### `extract_view_id_name.py`
   215ÔćĺExtracts unique `(id, name)` pairs from `view.fields` across all items in a
   216ÔćĺJSON or NDJSON file.
   217Ôćĺ
   218Ôćĺ- **Input**: any JSON array or NDJSON file of item objects
   219Ôćĺ- **Output**: JSON array of `[id, name]` tuples, one per line, sorted by id
   220Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/extract_view_id_name.py <input> <output>`
   221Ôćĺ- **Example**:
   222Ôćĺ  ```
   223Ôćĺ  python scripts/extract_view_id_name.py data/items-all-goethe-faust.json output/view_id_name.json
   224Ôćĺ  ```
   225Ôćĺ
   226Ôćĺ### `extract_view_fields.py`
   227ÔćĺExtracts and pretty-prints `view.fields` for a single item by ID.
   228Ôćĺ
   229Ôćĺ- **Input**: `data/items/<item-id>.json`
   230Ôćĺ- **Output**: `view-<item-id>.json` (project root)
   231Ôćĺ- **Usage**: `python scripts/extract_view_fields.py <item-id>`
   232Ôćĺ- **Example**:
   233Ôćĺ  ```
   234Ôćĺ  python scripts/extract_view_fields.py 222SF6AM6ZCXSJTNREHJUCX5SRTJDBK7
   235Ôćĺ  ```
   236Ôćĺ
   237Ôćĺ---
   238Ôćĺ
   239Ôćĺ## Data-collection scripts
   240Ôćĺ
   241Ôćĺ### `fetch-items.sh`
   242ÔćĺFetches DDB item records by ID from the DDB API, saves each as an individual
   243ÔćĺJSON file, and appends compact single-line JSON to the JSONL file. Skips IDs
   244Ôćĺalready present in the JSONL. Must be run from the `data/` directory.
   245Ôćĺ
   246Ôćĺ- **Input**: `<ids-file>` (one ID per line), optional `[limit]` count
   247Ôćĺ- **Output**: `data/items/<uuid>.json` per record; appends to
   248Ôćĺ  `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
   249Ôćĺ- **Usage**:
   250Ôćĺ  ```
   251Ôćĺ  cd data && bash ../scripts/fetch-items.sh ids-all-goethe-faust.txt
   252Ôćĺ  cd data && bash ../scripts/fetch-items.sh ids-missing.txt   # re-fetch gap
   253Ôćĺ  ```
   254Ôćĺ- **Notes**: 0.2 s delay between requests. HTTP failures are logged but do not
   255Ôćĺ  abort the run; failed IDs can be re-fetched by running again.
   256Ôćĺ
   257Ôćĺ### `find_missing_items.py`
   258ÔćĺCompares `ids-all-goethe-faust.txt` against `items-all-goethe-faust.json` and
   259Ôćĺwrites the gap ÔÇö IDs present in the list but absent from the JSONL ÔÇö to
   260Ôćĺ`ids-missing.txt` for targeted re-fetching.
   261Ôćĺ
   262Ôćĺ- **Input**: `data/ids-all-goethe-faust.txt`, `data/items-all-goethe-faust.json`
   263Ôćĺ- **Output**: `data/ids-missing.txt`
   264Ôćĺ- **Usage**:
   265Ôćĺ  ```
   266Ôćĺ  python scripts/find_missing_items.py
   267Ôćĺ  cd data && bash ../scripts/fetch-items.sh ids-missing.txt
   268Ôćĺ  ```
   269Ôćĺ
   270Ôćĺ### `fetch-progress.sh`
   271ÔćĺShell script to monitor fetch progress.
   272Ôćĺ



```
